content.content:
1	---
2	artifact_type: review-synthesis
3	method: flux-review
4	target: "docs/brainstorms/2026-04-19-model-then-decide-invert-brainstorm.md"
5	target_description: "Texturaize pivot — classify-then-rewrite → model-then-decide (v1 failure-mode aware + try-every-time attempts)"
6	tracks: 4
7	track_a_agents: [fd-active-learning-signal-design, fd-hci-assisted-writing, fd-llm-agent-architecture, fd-nlp-evaluation, fd-copy-editing-practice]
8	track_b_agents: [fd-medical-residency-apprentice-correction, fd-radiology-cad-disagreement-loop, fd-literary-translation-pairing-voice-fidelity, fd-aircraft-maintenance-trust-hierarchy]
9	track_c_agents: [fd-murano-prova-master-apprentice-correction, fd-polynesian-etak-moving-island-model, fd-quipu-knotkeeper-training-density, fd-tidal-estuary-sediment-equilibrium]
10	track_d_agents: [fd-tidal-harmonic-constituent-decomposition, fd-navajo-weaving-apprentice-taste-transmission, fd-mars-rover-tactical-sol-planning]
11	date: 2026-04-19
12	---
13	
14	# Model-Then-Decide Invert — Unified Review Synthesis
15	
16	## Executive Summary
17	
18	The pivot's UX thesis — attempts + alternatives, elicitation as product — survives adversarial review. The **learning-loop substrate beneath it does not**. Across all four tracks, 9 of 16 agents independently identified the same structural failure: Decision 3's "UI default: accept" combined with Decision 4's equal-weight three-channel harvest produces a training pipeline whose modal signal is fatigue-accept noise, not editorial judgment. Decision 2's v1 model is a passage-level log, not the piece-level "model" the pivot claims to build — it cannot represent cross-paragraph structure, cannot couple paragraph-level detection to piece intent, and reuses a 5-mode taxonomy that was never orthogonality-checked against the corpus. The highest-leverage single change before Phase 3 capture: **add provenance + dwell-time + engagement fields to every training row, and separate expert-MCP from non-expert-Scout signals at the data-pool level, before any training rows are written**. Without that, the learning loop is architecturally unclosable regardless of how correct the UX is.
19	
20	## Critical Findings (P0/P1)
21	
22	### 1. "UI default: accept" actively destroys the training signal it claims to generate [4/4 tracks]
23	
24	**Surfaced by:** fd-hci-assisted-writing (A), fd-active-learning-signal-design (A), fd-medical-residency-apprentice-correction (B), fd-radiology-cad-disagreement-loop (B), fd-murano-prova-master-apprentice-correction (C), fd-quipu-knotkeeper-training-density (C), fd-tidal-estuary-sediment-equilibrium (C), fd-navajo-weaving-apprentice-taste-transmission (D)
25	
26	**Problem:** Decision 3's default-accept primitive + Decision 4's treating accepts as positive signal creates a system that trains on inertia. HCI research (fd-hci-assisted-writing) shows professional override rates collapse to 15-30% of stated-intent within 4-6 sessions under default-accept UX. Radiology CAD research (fd-radiology-cad-disagreement-loop) shows the same pattern: at 1.5+ FP per image, radiologist behavior shifts to accepting dismissals as default — the CAD trains the reviewer to look where it points. The Muranese prova tradition (fd-murano-prova-master-apprentice-correction) names this as "silent set-aside" — the failure mode where apprentices' mediocre work is implicitly accepted, producing faster surface mimicry and slower structural internalization. Ryan's Scout-batch sessions past item 7-10 will produce rows that are structurally indistinguishable from fatigue noise, and the architecture has no way to filter them.
27	
28	The compound case is worse: fd-tidal-estuary-sediment-equilibrium identifies that non-expert + walk-away sessions produce maximum training row count with zero information content — the training pipeline will read them as "perfect session."
29	
30	**Fix:** Every training row must carry `{dwell_ms, engagement_type, queue_position, surface_origin, user_tier}`. Accepts with dwell <2-3s on a paragraph with a detected failure mode are demoted to an "untrusted-accept" bucket that is either excluded from training entirely or treated as very-low-weight SFT-warmup only. Zero-engagement sessions (no reject clicks, no alternative views, no free-text) are tagged `engagement: "none"` and excluded from training at pipeline level. **This instrumentation must be recorded at decision time — retrofitting is a schema migration and all rows written before the fix is in are permanently lossy.**
31	
32	### 2. Free-text corrections are not DPO-compatible as drafted — the richest signal will either be discarded or will poison the other channels [1/4, but load-bearing]
33	
34	**Surfaced by:** fd-active-learning-signal-design (A)
35	
36	**Problem:** Decision 4 classifies free-text corrections as "supervised (exact target to fit)" without specifying how this SFT signal composes with the DPO signal from accept/reject and pick-delta. DPO's implicit reward model requires the chosen completion to be drawn from the model's own output distribution; Ryan's free-text correction is by construction out-of-distribution. If the training pipeline mixes free-text corrections into a DPO pair factory naively (treating them as "preferred" against the model's attempt as "rejected"), DPO will assign enormous gradient to tokens the model cannot generate, producing mode collapse or divergence. The highest-signal channel is silently discarded or actively poisons the others.
37	
38	This is single-track but surfaces a catastrophic implementation landmine the other tracks couldn't see because they lack DPO-specific training-method knowledge. It is P0 because it converts Decision 4 from "we have a learning loop" to "we have a data-collection project" unless resolved.
39	
40	**Fix:** Specify training objective per channel before build: (a) accept/reject → Bradley-Terry reward model on attempt vs. original-passage pair; (b) pick-delta → DPO pair (both completions in-distribution by construction); (c) free-text → SFT with rejection-of-attempt as negative in separate loss head, OR rejection-sampled SFT that filters free-text to corrections a larger teacher can approximately reproduce. State explicitly in Phase 3 capture that the three channels do not blend into a single preference-pair pipeline.
41	
42	### 3. The "model of the piece" is a log-of-per-paragraph-detections, not a navigational position [4/4 tracks]
43	
44	**Surfaced by:** fd-copy-editing-practice (A), fd-polynesian-etak-moving-island-model (C), fd-navajo-weaving-apprentice-taste-transmission (D), fd-literary-translation-pairing-voice-fidelity (B), fd-tidal-harmonic-constituent-decomposition (D)
45	
46	**Problem:** Decision 2's schema is flat per-paragraph diagnostics plus a 1-2 sentence `intent`. It contains:
47	- no slot for cross-paragraph relations (merge candidates, argument contradictions, conclusion misplacement)
48	- no coupling mechanism between paragraph-level failure-mode detection and the `intent` field
49	- no piece-level voice aggregate (so compound voice drift where every paragraph clusters at TTR 0.44-0.46 is invisible)
50	- no session-glossary: decisions on paragraph 3 do not constrain the model's attempt on paragraph 15 (fd-literary-translation-pairing-voice-fidelity)
51	- no residual channel: paragraphs outside the 5-mode basis are silently marked clean, and the architecture has no mechanism to detect that its constituent set is incomplete (fd-tidal-harmonic-constituent-decomposition)
52	
53	fd-polynesian-etak-moving-island-model states this most sharply: the v1 schema is structurally a log-of-bearings, not a position estimate. A model trained on a schema that cannot represent cross-paragraph relations cannot develop cross-paragraph judgment from that training data, regardless of training volume.
54	
55	fd-copy-editing-practice confirms from editorial craft: a real copy editor models (1) what the piece IS, (2) what the piece DOES, (3) what the piece SOUNDS LIKE, then (4) diagnoses passages against the whole-piece model. The current schema flattens 2-4 into 4, which is editorially the same as the detectors-for-rewriting architecture the pivot claims to be leaving behind — just with a richer detector taxonomy.
56	
57	**Fix:** Before committing Decision 2, add four schema fields:
58	- `structuralRelations: [{paragraphs: [int], type: enum, reason: string, attempt: string|null}]` — cross-paragraph judgments (merge/split/reorder/cut)
59	- `voiceProfile: {ttrMean, ttrStdDev, burstinessMean, burstinessStdDev, ...}` — piece-level aggregates (compression of stdDev = flattened voice)
60	- `modelUncertain: bool, uncertainReason: string` — residual channel for paragraphs that deviate from genre baseline but match no named failure mode
61	- `sessionGlossary: Map<constructType, "endorsed"|"corrected">` — accumulated per-session constraint context, passed to subsequent paragraphs' LLM calls
62	
63	Couple `intent` to `diagnostic-context.ts` as a mandatory (not optional) conditioning input for all failure-mode detectors. If `intent` is null, detectors default to flagging ambiguities rather than making false-confident failure-mode calls.
64	
65	### 4. Non-expert signal contamination has no architectural firewall [3/4 tracks]
66	
67	**Surfaced by:** fd-hci-assisted-writing (A), fd-quipu-knotkeeper-training-density (C), fd-tidal-estuary-sediment-equilibrium (C), fd-murano-prova-master-apprentice-correction (C), fd-navajo-weaving-apprentice-taste-transmission (D)
68	
69	**Problem:** T4 acknowledges non-experts may accept uncritically, but Decision 3 commits to a single interaction shape. fd-quipu-knotkeeper-training-density identifies the structural cause: the architecture has no provenance mechanism to maintain the distinction between expert signal (Cuzco-trained keeper) and population noise (village keeper) as volume scales. By volume, non-expert signals will dominate; as the user base grows, Ryan's signal fraction approaches zero, and the classifier converges toward "whatever non-experts silently accept" — which is whatever the classifier's current output is. This is a self-reinforcing feedback loop invisible to per-session metrics.
70	
71	fd-tidal-estuary-sediment-equilibrium frames it as the depositional-baseline problem: MCP (tidal scouring) continues producing high-quality signal, but the baseline it is calibrating against drifts as Scout (riverine deposition) accumulates. The MCP signal does not degrade; the classifier's prior — what it attempts in the first place — shifts toward non-expert acceptance patterns.
72	
73	**Fix:** Every training row carries `user_tier: "expert-calibrator" | "expert" | "standard" | "unknown"` and `surface_origin: "mcp-chunk" | "scout-web"`. Minimum defensible policy: Ryan's signal weight 1.0; non-expert passive-accepts weight 0.0 until explicit calibration mechanism is built. Run separate training passes (or explicitly separate training weights) for each surface's data. Periodic held-out evaluation on Ryan's calibration corpus scores MCP-only classifier vs. MCP+Scout-pooled classifier — divergence on the held-out set is the contamination signal. Without this, no post-hoc detection path exists.
74	
75	### 5. No sample-efficiency target means "improvement" is unfalsifiable [2/4 tracks]
76	
77	**Surfaced by:** fd-active-learning-signal-design (A), fd-quipu-knotkeeper-training-density (C)
78	
79	**Problem:** T6 asks "Is 75-150 rows/session enough density?" and "What's the sample-efficiency profile of the planned training method?" The brainstorm names the question but proposes no answer. fd-quipu-knotkeeper-training-density makes this sequentially blocking: training methodology determines sample-efficiency profile, which determines whether 75-150 rows/session is sufficient, and the brainstorm commits to the architecture before answering any of the three. Without per-channel per-failure-mode sample targets, the team cannot distinguish "model is improving" from "model looks different between checkpoints." If the architecture needs 10,000 pairs per mode, that is 65+ Ryan-sessions (~13 weeks) per mode.
80	
81	**Fix:** Before Phase 3 capture, produce a one-page training-loop specification: (a) training methodology; (b) documented sample-efficiency profile for classifiers of this complexity; (c) minimum sessions-to-first-measurable-improvement at 75-150 rows/session; (d) hold-out 20% of Ryan's historical edits as calibration set with per-failure-mode accuracy targets. If estimated sessions-to-improvement exceeds 20 Ryan-sessions, the architecture must add data augmentation (teacher-model preference pairs) or the build must be scoped down. If this spec cannot be written, the pivot is premature.
82	
83	### 6. Independent per-paragraph attempts cascade into locally-correct/globally-incoherent outputs [2/4 tracks]
84	
85	**Surfaced by:** fd-copy-editing-practice (A), fd-polynesian-etak-moving-island-model (C)
86	
87	**Problem:** Decision 3 implies parallel generation of attempts against the original text. If Ryan accepts paragraph 2's attempt (which restructures a sentence), then accepts paragraph 3's attempt (generated in context of the ORIGINAL paragraph 2), the document now contains a structural discontinuity that was in neither the original nor either individual attempt. Track-changes UI shows this as accept-all success. Ryan does not see incoherence until re-reading.
88	
89	**Fix:** Attempts must be generated sequentially with rolling context — each attempt conditioned on previously-accepted edits. For Ryan's MCP flow, regenerate directly-affected downstream attempts after each accept event. For Scout batch, either accept this regression explicitly or serialize attempt presentation. Decide in Phase 3 capture.
90	
91	### 7. No RII-equivalent list — high-consequence edits auto-apply as mechanical [1/4 tracks, P0]
92	
93	**Surfaced by:** fd-aircraft-maintenance-trust-hierarchy (B)
94	
95	**Problem:** T8's mechanical/judgment boundary is drawn by difficulty (Vale `autoFix: true`), not by consequence. fd-aircraft-maintenance-trust-hierarchy shows from FAA Part 145 regulations that this exact category error — defining autonomy by action-type instead of by failure consequence — produces the Aloha Airlines pattern: skilled mechanics, simple tasks, catastrophic consequences of error. An `autoFix: true` rule that abbreviates named sources ("Dr. Sarah Chen, former CEO of Meridian Health" → "Dr. Chen") passes the difficulty test but fails the consequence test: attribution change, potential defamation risk, source credibility error, reportorial inaccuracy. The failure is non-recoverable by Ryan after submission.
96	
97	**Fix:** Define an RII (Required Inspection Item) list as regex patterns that force the judgment tier regardless of model failure-mode classification:
98	- Any change touching a number (`\b\d[\d,.]*\b`)
99	- Any change touching a proper noun in a noun phrase
100	- Any deletion of a title or credential (`\b(Dr|Prof|CEO|CFO|Director|Former|Ex)\b`)
101	- Any change to a date or year reference
102	
103	These run as deterministic post-model-output diff checks before tier assignment is finalized. Audit every `autoFix: true` rule in `rule-layers.ts` against consequence matrix — any rule touching proper nouns, numbers, dates, titles goes to judgment tier. This is an afternoon of work, not an architecture change.
104	
105	### 8. Revision-gate is architecturally invalid for the new output format [1/4 tracks, P0]
106	
107	**Surfaced by:** fd-nlp-evaluation (A)
108	
109	**Problem:** `packages/pipeline-worker/src/revision-gate.ts` implements 3-judge pillar scoring calibrated on fully-rewritten documents. The model-then-decide pivot produces a hybrid track-changes artifact (original passages + accepted attempts + user-picked alternatives + user free-text). Applying existing revision-gate to this will produce false negatives: excellent editorial sessions will fail the gate because the scoring rubric expects a particular output shape. The production quality gate silently stops working. The learning loop cannot use revision-gate scores to filter training signal.
110	
111	**Fix:** Before pivot ships, choose: (a) retire `revision-gate.ts` entirely and replace with per-attempt-quality scoring aggregated to session-level; or (b) keep `revision-gate.ts` for the "all attempts accepted" output only (measurable baseline floor), design separate rubric for hybrid artifact that factors user-decision distribution. Commit in Phase 3 capture.
112	
113	### 9. Failure-mode detection runs on pre-triaged text — model cannot develop independent detection capability [1/4 tracks]
114	
115	**Surfaced by:** fd-medical-residency-apprentice-correction (B)
116	
117	**Problem:** `packages/pipeline-worker/src/prelude.ts` runs Vale and CIPHER before the LLM sees the text. If the model builds its `failureModes` after seeing CIPHER's classification and Vale's flagged issues, it is not detecting `cutSpecifics` independently — it is confirming CIPHER's classification. This is the residency pre-triage deficit: residents from programs with heavy triage support underperform on the "order your own workup" board exam component. The model trains to be a CIPHER-confirmer, never develops the capability to detect `cutSpecifics` on raw input.
118	
119	**Fix:** For training signal, record both predictions separately: `{rawPrediction: FailureModeModel, preludedPrediction: FailureModeModel}`. The delta is training data for developing prelude-independent detection. Production path can still use prelude-informed predictions.
120	
121	## Cross-Track Convergence
122	
123	### Convergence 1 [4/4]: Default-accept destroys training signal
124	
125	**Already detailed as Critical Finding #1.** Different vocabularies:
126	- Track A (fd-hci-assisted-writing): "calibration gap" — measured override rate drops to 15-30% of stated-intent
127	- Track A (fd-active-learning-signal-design): "Goodhart loop on plausibility not correctness"
128	- Track B (fd-medical-residency-apprentice-correction): "silent agreement problem" — ACGME requires documented concurrence because accept-without-correction is the strongest positive signal the system has; must not be conflated with "never reviewed"
129	- Track B (fd-radiology-cad-disagreement-loop): "first-reader CAD anchoring bias" — the CAD mark shapes search patterns, not laziness
130	- Track C (fd-murano-prova-master-apprentice-correction): "silent set-aside" — the Muranese failure mode where the maestro defers diagnosis, producing surface mimicry
131	- Track C (fd-quipu-knotkeeper-training-density): "no within-expert variance measure" — Ryan's self-agreement rate on re-presented passages bounds the minimum detectable improvement
132	- Track C (fd-tidal-estuary-sediment-equilibrium): "compound walk-away + non-expert failure mode" — maximum row count, minimum information content
133	- Track D (fd-navajo-weaving-apprentice-taste-transmission): "no withhold channel" — training signal is always conditioned on an attempt having been made; the model never gets a signal for "considered attempting and chose not to"
134	
135	**Unified finding:** The architecture treats "signal generated" as equivalent to "signal informative." Every discipline that has operated a similar loop has learned that the two diverge under realistic conditions (fatigue, non-expert users, async review, inertia). Without provenance, dwell-time, engagement-type instrumentation, the three-channel harvest's "3x density" claim is 3x-noise claim.
136	
137	**Unified fix:** Every training row carries `{user_id, session_id, piece_id, paragraph_idx, attempt_id, action, alternatives_shown, alternative_picked, dwell_ms, queue_position, user_tier, surface_origin, attempt_model_confidence, rules_shown, signal_type: "agreement"|"correction"|"rejection", training_weight, engagement_type}`. This is the minimum schema to make any channel usable for training. Record at decision time; retrofitting is a schema migration that loses all previously-written rows.
138	
139	### Convergence 2 [4/4]: The v1 model is a log, not a model
140	
141	**Already detailed as Critical Finding #3.** Different vocabularies:
142	- Track A (fd-copy-editing-practice): "90% passage-diagnostics, 10% piece gesture — professionally misrepresented as 'model of the piece'"
143	- Track A (fd-llm-agent-architecture): "implementation will require at least 4 distinct LLM calls with different context requirements; conflating them into 'one big call' guarantees diagnostic-anchored attempts"
144	- Track B (fd-literary-translation-pairing-voice-fidelity): "missing session glossary — chapter 1 decisions do not constrain chapter 15"
145	- Track C (fd-polynesian-etak-moving-island-model): "log-of-bearings, not a position estimate — cannot detect internal inconsistency"
146	- Track D (fd-tidal-harmonic-constituent-decomposition): "no residual channel — architecture commits to never looking at what the 5 constituents don't explain"
147	- Track D (fd-navajo-weaving-apprentice-taste-transmission): "wrong unit of compositional judgment — paragraph is the typographic unit, not the compositional unit"
148	
149	**Unified finding:** The architecture optimizes for what is measurable at the paragraph level and structurally forecloses what requires piece-level or cross-paragraph representation. This is the same class of error as pre-Doodson tidal analysts using only M2 and S2. The scope-reduction to paragraph-level is not a neutral v1 simplification — it is a commitment to a training signal that cannot later develop structural or piece-level judgment from its own data.
150	
151	**Unified fix:** Add the four schema fields (`structuralRelations`, `voiceProfile`, `modelUncertain`, `sessionGlossary`). Couple `intent` to detection as mandatory conditioning. Do not ship v1 claiming "model of the piece" if the schema is 90% passage-diagnostics — either rewrite Decision 2 to include piece-level representation, or explicitly scope v1 as "passage-level only" with documented acknowledgment that structural/register/cross-paragraph pathologies will be systematically missed.
152	
153	### Convergence 3 [3/4]: The 5 failure modes are not operationally orthogonal
154	
155	**Surfaced by:** fd-copy-editing-practice (A), fd-nlp-evaluation (A), fd-tidal-harmonic-constituent-decomposition (D)
156	
157	**Different vocabularies:**
158	- Track A (fd-nlp-evaluation): "multi-class LLM classifiers on overlapping categories typically show 15-20% within-category confusion"
159	- Track A (fd-copy-editing-practice): "5 modes omit register failure and structural failure — two of the most common AI-output pathologies in long-form journalism"
160	- Track D (fd-tidal-harmonic-constituent-decomposition): "flattenedVoice and clicheSwap are near-degenerate tidal constituents — both fire when AI-generated text replaces vivid specific language with smooth generic language; the list was derived from editorial experience not from variance-decomposition of the actual corpus"
161	
162	**Unified finding:** Ryan's 5 failure modes are plausible starting categories but were never orthogonality-checked. They miss register failure and structural failure (the two most common long-form AI pathologies), they overlap pairwise (flattenedVoice × clicheSwap, cutSpecifics × tooConservative), and every mode can be scored "not detected" by under-editing — so the v1 model actively rewards the `tooConservative` failure mode it claims to detect.
163	
164	**Unified fix:** Before freezing Decision 2:
165	1. Run co-occurrence analysis on existing failure-mode labels in the corpus — if flattenedVoice × clicheSwap correlation >0.3, collapse them into compound `voiceErosion` with severity scalar
166	2. Make failure-mode fields severity scalars (float in [0,1] with null = not detected) instead of booleans — this gives the attempt-engine triage ordering and the training loop regression targets
167	3. Add a per-piece "editing aggressiveness" counter alongside failure-mode counts — a session with 0 detected failures AND 0 attempts is flagged as suspicious rather than clean
168	4. Either add register + structural failure modes (making it 7) or explicitly scope v1 as passage-level only
169	
170	### Convergence 4 [3/4]: The attempt schema lacks the model's reasoning
171	
172	**Surfaced by:** fd-murano-prova-master-apprentice-correction (C), fd-mars-rover-tactical-sol-planning (D), fd-radiology-cad-disagreement-loop (B)
173	
174	**Different vocabularies:**
175	- Track B (fd-radiology-cad-disagreement-loop): "spatial-level disagreement signal — relocation distance quantifies localization error"
176	- Track C (fd-murano-prova-master-apprentice-correction): "pick-up-the-punty correction — the pedagogical content was the demonstration of the causal chain, not just the result"
177	- Track D (fd-mars-rover-tactical-sol-planning): "no contingency pre-plan — rejection triggers a restart, not a cascade; attempts carry no `rejectionReasoning` or `contingencyAttempt` fields"
178	
179	**Unified finding:** The architecture optimizes the happy path (acceptance) and underspecifies the rejection path. When Ryan rejects an attempt, he faces A/B/C alternatives without the model's reasoning about why the attempt was wrong, forcing him to do more cognitive work post-rejection than on acceptance. The training loop inherits this asymmetry: a pick or correction records "model's attempt was wrong" without recording what failure mode triggered the disagreement.
180	
181	**Unified fix:**
182	- Attempt schema includes `attemptReasoning: string` (1-2 sentences: what problem this attempt is solving) and `contingencyHint: string` (if this seems wrong, likely X), displayed on rejection only
183	- After a pick or free-text, one-click "reason for disagreement" selector from the failure-mode set plus "other" — converts pick-delta rows from "B was preferred" into "B was preferred, correcting flattenedVoice"
184	- Record span-level diff between model attempt and Ryan's correction (computable client-side, no additional Ryan interaction) — CAD's localization signal
185	
186	### Convergence 5 [3/4]: Cross-session drift has no detection mechanism
187	
188	**Surfaced by:** fd-medical-residency-apprentice-correction (B), fd-aircraft-maintenance-trust-hierarchy (B), fd-tidal-estuary-sediment-equilibrium (C)
189	
190	**Different vocabularies:**
191	- Track B (fd-medical-residency-apprentice-correction): "no M&M conference equivalent — correction signals never aggregate to surface systematic gaps"
192	- Track B (fd-aircraft-maintenance-trust-hierarchy): "normalization of deviance — per-decision accepts accumulate into document-level drift without a cross-session audit; need C-check/D-check equivalent"
193	- Track C (fd-tidal-estuary-sediment-equilibrium): "composed-baseline quality has no periodic re-evaluation mechanism — degradation accumulates below per-interaction detection threshold"
194	
195	**Unified finding:** The learning loop monitors quality per-interaction but has no multi-session quality instrument. A 5% classifier degradation across 20 sessions is invisible in per-session reject rates (which fluctuate 15-25% from within-session variance). Without aggregate pattern detection, the training-signal density question is unanswerable because the team doesn't know what the rows are telling them.
196	
197	**Unified fix:**
198	1. Weekly `FailureModeReport` aggregation query over training-signal rows: per mode, `{falsePositiveRate, falseNegativeRate, genreBreakdown, lastImprovedAt}`. Runs scheduled on zklw. Output is the M&M conference agenda.
199	2. Calibration corpus: 20-30 passages with Ryan's canonical edits, collected during highest-attention sessions. Every 2 weeks, score current classifier against it. Drift >5% from baseline triggers re-calibration session.
200	3. Monthly normalization-of-deviance audit: take last 30 accepted pieces, compute aggregate `StatisticalProfile`, compare to baseline from first 30. Drift >1.5 SD flags potential accumulated-acceptance drift.
201	
202	### Convergence 6 [2/4]: No withhold / demonstration training channel
203	
204	**Surfaced by:** fd-navajo-weaving-apprentice-taste-transmission (D), fd-active-learning-signal-design (A)
205	
206	**Different vocabularies:**
207	- Track A (fd-active-learning-signal-design): "detection-gated sampling policy systematically starves signal on false negatives — no detection → no attempt → no training row → no signal to correct the blind spot"
208	- Track D (fd-navajo-weaving-apprentice-taste-transmission): "no withhold channel — the model never receives a signal for 'considered attempting and chose not to'; grandmother's silence over correct work is load-bearing pedagogy"
209	
210	**Unified finding:** The three channels are all conditioned on an attempt having been made. The model has no training pressure to leave correct passages alone. Over enough sessions the model learns an asymmetric lesson: surface more attempts (each accepted attempt is positive reward). The piece will be over-marked.
211	
212	**Unified fix:** Add `withheld_attempts: [{span, failureModeProbs, ryanLeftAlone: bool}]` to session-level training artifact. Also add periodic forced-surfacing: on ~10% of paragraphs (oversampling those where model reports "no failures"), generate an attempt + A/B/C even when detector says clean. Ryan's reject-all response is the only available source of false-negative signal.
213	
214	### Convergence 7 [2/4]: Voice drift detection is miscoupled
215	
216	**Surfaced by:** fd-llm-agent-architecture (A), fd-copy-editing-practice (A), fd-literary-translation-pairing-voice-fidelity (B)
217	
218	**Different vocabularies:**
219	- Track A (fd-llm-agent-architecture): "LLMs systematically misread raw statistical metrics — treat 'burstiness: 0.4' as quality judgment, then 'correct' voice drift by writing more uniformly"
220	- Track A (fd-copy-editing-practice): "voiceDrift is orphaned — computed but not routed to any attempt"
221	- Track B (fd-literary-translation-pairing-voice-fidelity): "flattenedVoice is binary — reviser's correction cannot close the loop because the flag does not identify what was flattened"
222	
223	**Unified fix:** (1) Never expose raw TTR/burstiness floats to LLM — quantize to categorical tags relative to corpus baseline (`above_baseline|at_baseline|below_baseline`). (2) Couple voiceDrift explicitly to flattenedVoice with evidence field: `flattenedVoice: {detected: bool, evidence: StatisticalDeltaEntry[] | null}` where evidence records which metrics crossed threshold by how much. (3) Post-session back-translation test: `computeStatisticalDelta(original, accepted-aggregate)`, alert if any metric moves >2 SD from original baseline.
224	
225	## Tradeoff Adjudication (T1-T8)
226	
227	### T1: Attempt-composes-baseline vs. 04-07 invariant
228	
229	**Adjudicated by:** fd-hci-assisted-writing, fd-copy-editing-practice, fd-nlp-evaluation, fd-murano-prova-master-apprentice-correction, fd-tidal-estuary-sediment-equilibrium, fd-navajo-weaving-apprentice-taste-transmission, fd-medical-residency-apprentice-correction, fd-aircraft-maintenance-trust-hierarchy
230	
231	**Consensus:** The softened invariant ("passable IF writer does nothing actively, because defaults apply") is professionally unusual and dangerous. fd-copy-editing-practice: a piece auto-edited by accepting every surfaced decision is "not a copy-edited piece — it is a pre-final machine draft with editor's veto unused." fd-hci-assisted-writing: walk-away-and-come-back IS the zero-interaction floor, and if it is not observably better than current classify-then-rewrite output, the pivot has a floor regression the decision-surface value cannot compensate.
232	
233	**Resolution:** Revise the invariant. All auto-accepted attempts are "auditable as a composed edit, NOT a copy-edited piece." Surface in UI with banner ("AI's best guess applied wholesale — review before publishing"). Pre-build gate: run one Scout-equivalent batch through simulated "model-then-decide auto-apply all" and measure against current classify-then-rewrite output on Ryan's rubric. If not strictly better, the pivot has a floor regression that must be addressed before ship. Also add `documentLevelAccept` event (fd-aircraft-maintenance-trust-hierarchy) — Ryan submitting the piece as complete is a categorically distinct training signal from accumulated per-decision accepts.
234	
235	### T2: Confidence calibration is still a thing
236	
237	**Adjudicated by:** fd-active-learning-signal-design, fd-hci-assisted-writing, fd-radiology-cad-disagreement-loop, fd-medical-residency-apprentice-correction, fd-tidal-harmonic-constituent-decomposition
238	
239	**Consensus:** The brainstorm is correct to reject confidence-gated surfacing, but fails to replace it with any alternative. fd-radiology-cad-disagreement-loop: CAD solved this not with confidence gating but with sensitivity tuning — per-failure-mode thresholds calibrated from training corpus false-positive rates. fd-medical-residency-apprentice-correction: externally-computed entrustment level (1-5 per failure mode per genre) is not self-referential and replaces the need for internal confidence. fd-tidal-harmonic-constituent-decomposition: severity is distinct from confidence; binary flags conflate them.
240	
241	**Resolution:**
242	- Replace boolean failure-mode fields with severity scalars in [0,1] (`null` = not detected)
243	- Implement per-failure-mode sensitivity dial in `diagnostic-context.ts`: `maxSurfacedPerPiece` per mode per genre, set by corpus calibration not by model confidence
244	- Compute `entrustmentLevel: 1-5` per attempt from the model's correction rate for that failure mode in that genre (external, not self-reported). UI treatment differs by level without exposing confidence scores.
245	- For high-uncertainty attempts (multi-mode overlap, or entrustment 1-2), force-review UX: not auto-applied — user must explicitly accept/reject/pick
246	
247	### T3: Scout / web UI path
248	
249	**Adjudicated by:** fd-hci-assisted-writing, fd-medical-residency-apprentice-correction, fd-quipu-knotkeeper-training-density, fd-tidal-estuary-sediment-equilibrium, fd-mars-rover-tactical-sol-planning
250	
251	**Consensus:** The web UI is not just a product strategy question — it is an operational necessity question. fd-mars-rover-tactical-sol-planning: Scout's async-review regime cannot function without triage infrastructure; 200 pieces × 15-30 undifferentiated attempts is telemetry without decisional downlink. fd-hci-assisted-writing: acceptance rate climbs to 100% by item 8-12 in batch review; Scout pieces past item 5 are fatigue-degraded signal.
252	
253	**Resolution:** The web UI requires a decisional-downlink artifact: a `reviewSummary` field in the model-of-the-piece with top 3-5 highest-severity judgment calls ranked by severity, rendered above-the-fold before the full track-changes document. Scout training signal must be architecturally separated from MCP: `surface_origin` field on every row, separate training runs or weights, held-out calibration evaluation detects cross-surface contamination. Also required: either drop Scout from training signal entirely (`training_weight: 0`) or require minimum engagement per judgment call past item 5 (force-slow throughput loss for signal integrity). Decide before build.
254	
255	### T4: Non-expert writers
256	
257	**Adjudicated by:** fd-hci-assisted-writing, fd-quipu-knotkeeper-training-density, fd-tidal-estuary-sediment-equilibrium, fd-murano-prova-master-apprentice-correction, fd-navajo-weaving-apprentice-taste-transmission
258	
259	**Consensus:** Unified interaction model across expert and non-expert is the primary contamination risk for paying-customer era. fd-hci-assisted-writing: calibration gap in non-experts systematically over-trusts AI outputs in professional-seeming UX regardless of labeling. fd-quipu-knotkeeper-training-density: without tier provenance, as user base grows Ryan's signal fraction approaches zero and the classifier converges toward non-expert acceptance patterns.
260	
261	**Resolution:** Tier users explicitly at registration. Expert (Ryan + invited editors) → full try-every-time UX, training weight 1.0. Non-expert (paying customers) → force-review UX (no auto-apply, explicit accept required per decision), training weight 0.0 until verified-editorial flag is earned. Sessions with accept-rate >95% flagged as low-trust. Write tier into training-row schema. This preserves the non-expert product without letting their signal contaminate the preference model.
262	
263	### T5: v1 model coverage
264	
265	**Adjudicated by:** fd-nlp-evaluation, fd-copy-editing-practice, fd-llm-agent-architecture, fd-polynesian-etak-moving-island-model, fd-tidal-harmonic-constituent-decomposition, fd-navajo-weaving-apprentice-taste-transmission
266	
267	**Consensus:** The failure-mode-aware schema shifts Goodhart rather than solving it. Every mode has a "not detected = under-edited" path. The 5 modes omit register and structural failures. The model is a log of per-paragraph detections, not a piece-level model.
268	
269	**Resolution:** See Critical Finding #3 + Convergence 3. Concrete:
270	- Add severity scalars (not booleans) to failure modes
271	- Add `modelUncertain` residual channel
272	- Add `structuralRelations` array at top level
273	- Add `voiceProfile` piece-level aggregate
274	- Couple `intent` as mandatory conditioning for all detectors
275	- Either add register + structural modes or scope v1 explicitly as passage-level only
276	- Add per-piece editing-aggressiveness counter as Goodhart-resistance metric (0 attempts + 0 failures = suspicious flag)
277	
278	### T6: Learning loop calibration requires Ryan's time
279	
280	**Adjudicated by:** fd-active-learning-signal-design, fd-quipu-knotkeeper-training-density, fd-medical-residency-apprentice-correction, fd-nlp-evaluation
281	
282	**Consensus:** The question is not density but (a) what training method, (b) what sample-efficiency profile for that method, (c) what calibration-corpus target. The three are sequentially blocking. Without answers, density is a village-accounting count being applied to an imperial-pattern-detection question.
283	
284	**Resolution:** See Critical Finding #5. Non-negotiable pre-build: one-page training-loop spec with training method, per-channel objective, per-mode sample target, held-out calibration protocol. If the spec estimates >20 Ryan-sessions to first measurable improvement, either add data augmentation or scope v1 down.
285	
286	### T7: The attempt might be the wrong unit
287	
288	**Adjudicated by:** fd-copy-editing-practice, fd-polynesian-etak-moving-island-model, fd-navajo-weaving-apprentice-taste-transmission, fd-literary-translation-pairing-voice-fidelity, fd-tidal-harmonic-constituent-decomposition
289	
290	**Consensus:** The attempt unit is wrong — and the consequence of shipping v1 at the wrong unit is that training data is locked to passage-level, structurally foreclosing cross-paragraph judgment. fd-navajo-weaving-apprentice-taste-transmission: scope reductions that exclude signal types from the training loop are irreversible in ways UI scope reductions are not.
291	
292	**Resolution:** Add `structuralRelations: [{paragraphs: [int], type: "merge"|"split"|"reorder"|"cut", reasoning: string, attempt: string | null}]` at top level of model-of-the-piece. For v1, populate only when failure-mode pass detects high voiceDrift variance across consecutive paragraphs OR when loadBearing elements in consecutive paragraphs reference each other. Cheap v1 population, but critically: training rows start accumulating at structural granularity from day one, so the model is not cold-start for structural judgment when v2 expands scope.
293	
294	### T8: Mechanical vs. judgment fix boundary
295	
296	**Adjudicated by:** fd-aircraft-maintenance-trust-hierarchy, fd-llm-agent-architecture, fd-copy-editing-practice, fd-tidal-estuary-sediment-equilibrium, fd-mars-rover-tactical-sol-planning
297	
298	**Consensus:** Strong convergence across 5 agents, three distinct mechanisms. fd-aircraft-maintenance-trust-hierarchy: consequence-based RII list (the most directly actionable finding in the review — afternoon of work). fd-llm-agent-architecture: mechanical is a closed code enumeration, not an LLM prompt. fd-copy-editing-practice: sentence splitting is ALWAYS a judgment call regardless of length; "grammar" in the broad sense is not mechanical. fd-tidal-estuary-sediment-equilibrium: context-dependent borderline cases need surface-as-judgment default. fd-mars-rover-tactical-sol-planning: operational criteria per instance (confidence + reversibility + impact-if-wrong), not category-level auto-apply.
299	
300	**Resolution:**
301	1. Mechanical bucket is a closed code enumeration (TypeScript const in `rule-layers.ts`), strictly: spelling (dictionary), typographic standardization (smart quotes, en/em dashes, hyphens), Vale rules already tagged "deterministic substitution". Everything else — including sentence splitting, clause reordering, "grammar" in the broad sense — is a judgment call by construction.
302	2. Add RII pattern list that forces judgment-tier regardless of mechanical classification: any change touching numbers, proper nouns in noun phrases, titles/credentials, dates/years.
303	3. Per-edit confidence + reversibility + impact-if-wrong scoring in `rule-layers.ts` — auto-apply gated on `confidence > 0.95 AND impactIfWrong == 'low'`; everything else surfaces as judgment call (pre-selected for accept convenience, but visible and rejectable).
304	
305	## Track-Specific Insights
306	
307	### Domain-Expert Insights (Track A)
308	
309	**Training-objective specificity (fd-active-learning-signal-design):** Most specialists would reflexively say "three channels, train a preference model." This agent's DPO-compatibility analysis is the single most implementation-critical finding non-specialists could not have surfaced — it converts Decision 4 from "done" to "train on free-text as SFT with rejection-sampling filter, NOT as DPO-preferred completion." Three channels, three distinct training objectives, no blending.
310	
311	**LLM call-graph isolation (fd-llm-agent-architecture):** The brainstorm treats "model of the piece" as a single conceptual artifact. The implementation requires 4 distinct LLM calls with different context requirements. Conflating them guarantees diagnostic-anchored attempts (model anchors to its own diagnostic vocabulary rather than the piece's voice). Isolation boundary between diagnosis-call and generation-call is the most load-bearing architecture decision; one big schema-emitting call is the wrong choice even if it looks cheapest.
312	
313	**Evaluation infrastructure precedence (fd-nlp-evaluation):** Evaluation redesign must happen BEFORE the pivot, not in parallel. Existing `revision-gate.ts` is architecturally invalid for the hybrid output format. New failure-mode classifiers have no specified inter-rater reliability target — Krippendorff's alpha on 3-run detection of 10 paragraphs per mode (30 LLM calls, an hour of work) must happen before schema is frozen. Modes with alpha <0.7 cannot be used as hard training labels.
314	
315	**Editorial craft reality (fd-copy-editing-practice):** The junior/senior editor metaphor misrepresents what the system is. A junior has global comprehension. The AI does not. A junior writes queries for ambiguities, not 15-30 decision points per piece — a real senior reviewing that volume of queries would conclude the junior is unqualified. Either change the framing to "deterministic passage-level reviewer" or re-architect to match the metaphor (piece-level comprehension, consolidated queries).
316	
317	**HCI UX vs. signal-validity (fd-hci-assisted-writing):** Decision 3 is really three different UX decisions (Ryan-MCP, Ryan-Scout, non-expert) compressed into one. Until Decision 3 decomposes by user/surface tier with explicit per-tier UX primitives and per-tier training weights, the "3x signal density" claim overstates available signal.
318	
319	### Parallel-Discipline Insights (Track B)
320	
321	**Silent agreement problem (fd-medical-residency-apprentice-correction):** ACGME requires documented concurrence on accept-without-correction because it is the strongest positive evidence the system has — but only when it carries `dwellMs` and the failure modes that were presented. Add `reviewEvidence` field. Add the M&M-conference scheduled aggregation query on zklw.
322	
323	**CAD second-reader mode (fd-radiology-cad-disagreement-loop):** First-reader CAD anchors the radiologist's search pattern. Second-reader mode (original first, then attempt on demand) produces systematically higher-quality training rows. Opt-in per session for MCP, flagged in training schema as `{displayMode: "second-reader"}`, weighted higher in calibration. Plus differential weighting: disagreement rows get 3x weight vs. agreement rows (established CAD-FDA training protocol).
324	
325	**Translation glossary persistence (fd-literary-translation-pairing-voice-fidelity):** The most actionable structural missing piece. Chunk-by-chunk without within-session constraint propagation produces incoherent output even when each chunk is locally correct. `sessionGlossary` accumulator in MCP handler — small ephemeral state, passed as JSON context block to each subsequent paragraph's model call. Plus project-level voice document for A/B/C generation constraint (computed from Ryan's approved edits per author).
326	
327	**FAA Required Inspection Items (fd-aircraft-maintenance-trust-hierarchy):** The review's most directly actionable finding. T8 is framed as philosophical ("who decides?"); the aviation answer is operational — consequence-based RII list pre-decided by expert review. Audit `autoFix: true` rules in `rule-layers.ts` against consequence matrix. Afternoon of work, closes a catastrophic-failure-mode gap.
328	
329	### Structural Insights (Track C)
330	
331	**Diagnosis vs. correction capture (fd-murano-prova-master-apprentice-correction):** The architecture has designed a correction-capture system without a diagnosis-capture system. The pedagogical mechanism in prova was the diagnosis, not the correction. One-click "reason for disagreement" from the failure-mode set converts pick-delta from preference signal to diagnostic signal — without which the failure-mode classifier improves Ryan's pick-prediction while remaining structurally unable to improve its failure-mode detection.
332	
333	**Position estimate vs. log-of-bearings (fd-polynesian-etak-moving-island-model):** Names exactly what "model of the piece" aspires to be vs. what the v1 schema actually is. A log of per-paragraph detections cannot detect cross-paragraph inconsistency; a position estimate can. The distinction is architectural, not a scope question — no training volume on paragraph-level labels produces cross-paragraph judgment in a schema that has no slot for it.
334	
335	**Calibration hierarchy (fd-quipu-knotkeeper-training-density):** Signal quality is a property of both channel type AND provenance. The brainstorm decomposes by channel but not by provenance. User-tier + surface-origin provenance must be enforced at schema level, or as the user base grows the classifier converges toward "whatever non-experts silently accept" — which is whatever the classifier already produces. Self-reinforcing feedback loop invisible to per-session metrics. Plus: Ryan's within-expert variance is unmeasured; without a self-agreement test on re-presented passages, the noise floor of the accept/reject channel is unknown and minimum-detectable-improvement is undefined.
336	
337	**Cross-surface substrate sharing (fd-tidal-estuary-sediment-equilibrium):** Two processes (MCP + Scout) sharing a substrate (training pipeline) are defined by their interaction dynamics, not by the properties of either process alone. Per-surface metrics on MCP cannot detect the drift because Ryan's behavior is unchanged — what changes is the classifier's prior, shaped by the pooled training signal. Held-out evaluation comparing MCP-only vs. MCP+Scout-pooled classifiers on Ryan's calibration corpus is the only contamination detection instrument.
338	
339	### Frontier Patterns (Track D)
340	
341	**Residual channel as load-bearing (fd-tidal-harmonic-constituent-decomposition):** The deepest finding in the review. Coastal flood-warning systems monitor residuals, not fitted tides — structured residual reveals incomplete constituent sets, and discarding it kills people. The brainstorm's architecture commits to never looking at what the 5 failure modes don't explain. `modelUncertain: bool` field is a one-line schema change that converts the residual from discarded noise to a prioritized signal channel. Plus: the 5 modes were derived from editorial experience, not from variance-decomposition of the corpus — pairwise correlation analysis is a test that has never been run.
342	
343	**Spirit-line discipline as prescribed anti-optimization (fd-navajo-weaving-apprentice-taste-transmission):** Diné weaving encodes mandatory imperfection as a rule of the tradition — optimization toward a completeness metric is prescribed to have an escape hatch. Second-order Goodhart: the anti-Goodhart solution (voice metrics as diagnostic) is itself Goodhartable (model learns to preserve metrics rather than voice). Designate TTR and burstiness as `diagnosticOnly: true` — flag passages for Ryan's attention, never score attempts. Plus the withhold channel — demonstration rows ("Ryan left this alone") are as important as correction rows, and the architecture has no slot for them. Without them, the model develops a negative-space definition of quality (everything not rejected) rather than a positive definition; the ceiling is Ryan's rejection threshold, not Ryan's taste.
344	
345	**Rover decisional downlink (fd-mars-rover-tactical-sol-planning):** The most operationally specific finding from the outer tracks. Systems with latency between actor and decision-maker eventually invent: (a) contingency pre-planning (`attemptReasoning` + `contingencyHint` in the attempt schema, displayed only on rejection); (b) regime-specific interfaces (web UI needs `reviewSummary` triage artifact, not raw track-changes); (c) operational-criteria-based autonomy boundaries (per-instance confidence + reversibility + impact, not action-type categorization). Mars rovers had to invent all three on day one because of the 20-minute comm delay; Scout's async-review regime faces the same constraint. Plus: keep-out zones for `loadBearing` spans — hard exclusions registered in the attempt-engine before any attempt generation, not relying on `cutSpecifics` detector post-hoc.
346	
347	## Architectural Recommendations (Ordered)
348	
349	Lead with changes that, if not made, will cause the architecture to collapse.
350	
351	### P0 — Collapse Risks (must resolve before Phase 3 capture)
352	
353	1. **Full training-row schema with provenance, engagement, and signal-type columns — recorded from day one.** Convergence 1 + 4. Fields: `{user_id, session_id, piece_id, paragraph_idx, attempt_id, action, alternatives_shown, alternative_picked, dwell_ms, queue_position, user_tier, surface_origin, signal_type: "agreement"|"correction"|"rejection", training_weight, engagement_type, attempt_model_confidence, rules_shown, display_mode}`. Effort: 1 day schema + instrumentation. Depends-on: nothing. Retrofit is lossy — every row written before this fix is in is permanently unusable for quality-weighted training.
354	
355	2. **One-page training-loop specification.** Critical Finding #5. Commit to training methodology per channel, per-mode sample-efficiency target, held-out calibration protocol, 20% held-out from Ryan's historical edits. Effort: 2-3 days research + writing. Depends-on: Recommendation #1 schema. If spec cannot be written, pivot is premature.
356	
357	3. **Separate MCP and Scout training at the data-pool level.** Critical Finding #4 + Convergence T3/T4. Separate training runs OR explicit separate weights. Held-out calibration evaluation on Ryan's corpus compares MCP-only vs. MCP+Scout-pooled classifiers. Effort: 1 day data-plumbing + 1 day evaluation-script scaffold. Depends-on: Recommendation #1 (surface_origin field exists).
358	
359	4. **Decision 2 schema additions — before build.** Convergence 2 + T5. Four fields: `structuralRelations[]`, `voiceProfile` piece-level aggregate, `modelUncertain: bool`, `sessionGlossary` accumulator. Make `intent` mandatory conditioning for all detectors in `diagnostic-context.ts`. Replace boolean failure modes with severity scalars `[0,1]` (null = not detected). Effort: 2 days schema + prompt updates. Depends-on: nothing.
360	
361	5. **RII list audit of `rule-layers.ts`.** Critical Finding #7 + T8. Deterministic post-model-output diff check for numbers, proper nouns, titles, dates. Any `autoFix: true` rule touching these moves to judgment tier. Mechanical bucket becomes closed code enumeration (TypeScript const), not LLM judgment. Effort: afternoon. Depends-on: nothing.
362	
363	6. **Revision-gate redesign decision.** Critical Finding #8 + T1. Choose: retire entirely with per-attempt scoring OR keep for "all attempts accepted" output only with separate hybrid-artifact rubric. Before ship. Effort: 1-2 days to decide + scope; full redesign is separate work. Depends-on: Decision 2 schema finalized.
364	
365	### P1 — Architectural Improvements (needed but not collapse-inducing)
366	
367	7. **Isolated-context attempt generation.** fd-llm-agent-architecture. Attempt call receives only `{paragraph_text, failure_mode_tag, piece_intent}` — NOT the full model-of-the-piece JSON. Run contamination test (10 pieces, blind-rated shared-vs-isolated conditions) before locking decision. Effort: 1 day test + prompt refactor. Depends-on: #4.
368	
369	8. **Sequential attempt generation with rolling context.** Critical Finding #6. Each attempt conditioned on previously-accepted edits. For MCP, regenerate directly-affected downstream attempts after each accept event. Effort: 2-3 days (session state + regeneration logic). Depends-on: #1, #4.
370	
371	9. **Per-failure-mode sensitivity dial in `diagnostic-context.ts`.** fd-radiology-cad-disagreement-loop + T2. `maxSurfacedPerPiece` per mode per genre, calibrated from corpus false-positive rates. Requires one-time calibration pass (20 pieces from Ryan's edited corpus). Effort: 1 day calibration + 1 day implementation. Depends-on: #4 (severity scalars).
372	
373	10. **Withhold-attempt channel + forced-surfacing.** Convergence 6. Log `withheld_attempts: [{span, failureModeProbs, ryanLeftAlone}]` to session artifact. Force-surface attempt + A/B/C on 10% of "clean" paragraphs (budget against Ryan's throughput). Effort: 1 day logging + 1 day UX. Depends-on: #1.
374	
375	11. **Attempt schema reasoning + contingency + reason-for-disagreement.** Convergence 4. `attemptReasoning`, `contingencyHint` generated per attempt (collapsed UI, expanded on rejection). Post-pick/free-text one-click reason selector. Span-level diff between attempt and correction logged client-side. Effort: 2 days prompts + UI + schema. Depends-on: #1.
376	
377	12. **Non-expert tier UX separation.** T4 + Convergence 1. Expert tier: try-every-time auto-apply, weight 1.0. Non-expert tier: force-review (no auto-apply, explicit accept per decision), weight 0.0 until verified-editorial flag. Sessions with accept-rate >95% flagged low-trust. Effort: 1-2 days UX + tier logic. Depends-on: #1 (user_tier field).
378	
379	13. **Decisional-downlink `reviewSummary` for web UI.** Convergence T3 + fd-mars-rover-tactical-sol-planning. Top 3-5 highest-severity judgment calls ranked by severity scalar, above-the-fold card in web UI. Effort: 1-2 days. Depends-on: #4 (severity scalars), #9.
380	
381	14. **Second-reader display mode for MCP calibration sessions.** fd-radiology-cad-disagreement-loop. Opt-in per session. Training rows flagged `{displayMode: "second-reader"}`, weighted higher in calibration. Effort: 1 day MCP tool change. Depends-on: #1.

385	15. **Weekly FailureModeReport aggregation on zklw.** Convergence 5 + fd-medical-residency-apprentice-correction. Per-mode `{falsePositiveRate, falseNegativeRate, genreBreakdown, lastImprovedAt}`. Scheduled job. Effort: 1 day query + cron. Depends-on: #1.

392

396	
397	## What Holds Up
398	
399	The following pass adversarial review and can proceed:
400	
401	- **Decision 1 (Scope — Option C "Invert"):** Concurred by fd-copy-editing-practice, fd-llm-agent-architecture, fd-medical-residency-apprentice-correction, fd-mars-rover-tactical-sol-planning. Multiple agents explicitly endorsed abandoning silent rewrite as the correct scope choice. The explicit rejection of options A and B holds up — middle-path approaches would contaminate the learning signal and not address the pipeline-is-wrong-shape concern.
402	
403	- **Hard Constraint: Elicitation is the product.** No agent contested this. fd-mars-rover-tactical-sol-planning frames the entire rover operations experience as structurally confirming this — for asynchronous-review regimes, the decisional-downlink artifact (not the raw telemetry) is the product.
404	
405	- **Hard Constraint: No "recommended" label.** Agent findings refine this rather than contest it. fd-hci-assisted-writing notes the label does not prevent anchoring (which the brainstorm does not claim — it claims trust hasn't been earned yet, which is correct). The fix is display-order randomization and position-bias correction, not reverting to "recommended."
406	
407	- **Hard Constraint: No Opus in bulk runs.** Irrelevant to most agents; those that addressed compute cost (fd-llm-agent-architecture) endorsed prompt-caching at model-of-the-piece level and cheap short attempt calls. Cost envelope appears tractable.
408	
409	- **Ryan's 04-07 "internal prediction" invariant.** fd-active-learning-signal-design flags that internal-prediction-vs-user-pick delta is a distinct fourth training signal that the brainstorm conflates with the three channels. But the invariant itself — that prediction must be recorded — is validated as structurally necessary, not questioned.
410	
411	- **Decision 4's three-channel signal topology.** The channels themselves are the right channels. What's broken is (a) missing provenance + weight columns, (b) DPO-incompatibility of free-text, (c) absence of withhold channel. None of these require rethinking the three channels — they require extending the schema around them.
412	
413	- **Commitment to junior-editor framing for internal model self-conception.** fd-medical-residency-apprentice-correction confirms the pedagogical metaphor is correct. The refinements (silent agreement documentation, M&M aggregation, entrustment-level tracking, write-ups on rejection) extend the metaphor rather than reject it. The framing mismatch identified by fd-copy-editing-practice and fd-murano-prova-master-apprentice-correction applies to the USER-facing UX framing (which needs correction), not to the internal model's self-conception.
414	
415	## Synthesis Assessment
416	
417	**Overall quality of the brainstorm:** High on UX thesis clarity; medium on architectural specificity; low on training-loop substrate. The committed decisions (1-4) are well-framed and Decision 1's scope is correct. The v1 schema (Decision 2) is under-specified; the interaction shape (Decision 3) compresses three distinct user regimes into one; the training harvest (Decision 4) lists channels without any training-objective or weighting specification. The brainstorm is self-aware enough to list 8 open tradeoffs, but 6 of the 8 have resolutions already suggested by one or more agents — the tradeoffs are more tractable than their unresolved framing suggests.
418	
419	**Highest-leverage single improvement:** Full training-row schema with provenance, engagement, dwell-time, and signal-type columns recorded from day one. This is the single change that, if not made before any training rows are written, permanently degrades all subsequent data. Every other architectural improvement can be made later with higher cost but without data loss; this one cannot. It is simultaneously the smallest effort (1 day) and the highest leverage. It is the gating recommendation for Phase 3 capture.
420	
421	**Most surprising finding:** The residual channel from fd-tidal-harmonic-constituent-decomposition. The insight that storm-surge monitoring systems watch residuals not fitted signals, because residuals reveal incomplete constituent sets, is the one reframe no single adjacent-domain agent would produce. The brainstorm worries about Goodhart at the failure-mode detection level (T5) but does not realize that the architecture commits to never looking at what the 5 failure modes don't explain. `modelUncertain: bool` is a one-field schema change that converts discarded noise into the signal that reveals when the mode set itself is wrong. Equally surprising but second-place: the aircraft-maintenance RII list from fd-aircraft-maintenance-trust-hierarchy, which converts T8 from unresolved philosophy to an afternoon of deterministic regex work.
422	
423	**Semantic distance value:** The outer tracks earned their cost. Track C's quipu-keeper (provenance hierarchy) and etak (position vs. log) findings are not available from Tracks A or B — they require the structural isomorphism perspective of non-Western knowledge systems that operated at scale without modern instrumentation. Track D's tidal-harmonic residual analysis and Mars rover contingency pre-planning are genuinely novel frames that reframe Decision 2 and T3 respectively in ways no specialist would produce. Conversely, fd-navajo-weaving's ch'ihónít'i spirit-line is more restatement than new insight (the Goodhart-on-metrics concern appears in Tracks A and D), and fd-murano-prova's diagnosis-capture finding converges substantially with fd-medical-residency's M&M aggregation — the tradition-specific framings overlap more than they reveal new mechanisms. Overall: 4/7 outer-track agents produced findings that alter the architectural recommendations; 3/7 restated inner-track findings in novel vocabulary. That ratio justifies the outer-track investment — the "never would have thought of" category has three entries (residual channel, position-vs-log, RII list), each in a different track, each changing the recommendation list.
424

track_d_agents: [fd-tidal-harmonic-constituent-decomposition, fd-navajo-weaving-apprentice-taste-transmission, fd-mars-rover-tactical-sol-planning]
date: 2026-04-19
