# Model-Then-Decide Invert — Unified Review Synthesis

## Executive Summary

The pivot's UX thesis — attempts + alternatives, elicitation as product — survives adversarial review. The **learning-loop substrate beneath it does not**. Across all four tracks, 9 of 16 agents independently identified the same structural failure: Decision 3's "UI default: accept" combined with Decision 4's equal-weight three-channel harvest produces a training pipeline whose modal signal is fatigue-accept noise, not editorial judgment. Decision 2's v1 model is a passage-level log, not the piece-level "model" the pivot claims to build — it cannot represent cross-paragraph structure, cannot couple paragraph-level detection to piece intent, and reuses a 5-mode taxonomy that was never orthogonality-checked against the corpus. The highest-leverage single change before Phase 3 capture: **add provenance + dwell-time + engagement fields to every training row, and separate expert-MCP from non-expert-Scout signals at the data-pool level, before any training rows are written**. Without that, the learning loop is architecturally unclosable regardless of how correct the UX is.

## Critical Findings (P0/P1)

### 1. "UI default: accept" actively destroys the training signal it claims to generate [4/4 tracks]

**Surfaced by:** fd-hci-assisted-writing (A), fd-active-learning-signal-design (A), fd-medical-residency-apprentice-correction (B), fd-radiology-cad-disagreement-loop (B), fd-murano-prova-master-apprentice-correction (C), fd-quipu-knotkeeper-training-density (C), fd-tidal-estuary-sediment-equilibrium (C), fd-navajo-weaving-apprentice-taste-transmission (D)

**Problem:** Decision 3's default-accept primitive + Decision 4's treating accepts as positive signal creates a system that trains on inertia. HCI research (fd-hci-assisted-writing) shows professional override rates collapse to 15-30% of stated-intent within 4-6 sessions under default-accept UX. Radiology CAD research (fd-radiology-cad-disagreement-loop) shows the same pattern: at 1.5+ FP per image, radiologist behavior shifts to accepting dismissals as default — the CAD trains the reviewer to look where it points. The Muranese prova tradition (fd-murano-prova-master-apprentice-correction) names this as "silent set-aside" — the failure mode where apprentices' mediocre work is implicitly accepted, producing faster surface mimicry and slower structural internalization. Ryan's Scout-batch sessions past item 7-10 will produce rows that are structurally indistinguishable from fatigue noise, and the architecture has no way to filter them.

The compound case is worse: fd-tidal-estuary-sediment-equilibrium identifies that non-expert + walk-away sessions produce maximum training row count with zero information content — the training pipeline will read them as "perfect session."

**Fix:** Every training row must carry `{dwell_ms, engagement_type, queue_position, surface_origin, user_tier}`. Accepts with dwell <2-3s on a paragraph with a detected failure mode are demoted to an "untrusted-accept" bucket that is either excluded from training entirely or treated as very-low-weight SFT-warmup only. Zero-engagement sessions (no reject clicks, no alternative views, no free-text) are tagged `engagement: "none"` and excluded from training at pipeline level. **This instrumentation must be recorded at decision time — retrofitting is a schema migration and all rows written before the fix is in are permanently lossy.**

### 2. Free-text corrections are not DPO-compatible as drafted — the richest signal will either be discarded or will poison the other channels [1/4, but load-bearing]

**Surfaced by:** fd-active-learning-signal-design (A)

**Problem:** Decision 4 classifies free-text corrections as "supervised (exact target to fit)" without specifying how this SFT signal composes with the DPO signal from accept/reject and pick-delta. DPO's implicit reward model requires the chosen completion to be drawn from the model's own output distribution; Ryan's free-text correction is by construction out-of-distribution. If the training pipeline mixes free-text corrections into a DPO pair factory naively (treating them as "preferred" against the model's attempt as "rejected"), DPO will assign enormous gradient to tokens the model cannot generate, producing mode collapse or divergence. The highest-signal channel is silently discarded or actively poisons the others.

This is single-track but surfaces a catastrophic implementation landmine the other tracks couldn't see because they lack DPO-specific training-method knowledge. It is P0 because it converts Decision 4 from "we have a learning loop" to "we have a data-collection project" unless resolved.

**Fix:** Specify training objective per channel before build: (a) accept/reject → Bradley-Terry reward model on attempt vs. original-passage pair; (b) pick-delta → DPO pair (both completions in-distribution by construction); (c) free-text → SFT with rejection-of-attempt as negative in separate loss head, OR rejection-sampled SFT that filters free-text to corrections a larger teacher can approximately reproduce. State explicitly in Phase 3 capture that the three channels do not blend into a single preference-pair pipeline.

### 3. The "model of the piece" is a log-of-per-paragraph-detections, not a navigational position [4/4 tracks]

**Surfaced by:** fd-copy-editing-practice (A), fd-polynesian-etak-moving-island-model (C), fd-navajo-weaving-apprentice-taste-transmission (D), fd-literary-translation-pairing-voice-fidelity (B), fd-tidal-harmonic-constituent-decomposition (D)

**Problem:** Decision 2's schema is flat per-paragraph diagnostics plus a 1-2 sentence `intent`. It contains:
- no slot for cross-paragraph relations (merge candidates, argument contradictions, conclusion misplacement)
- no coupling mechanism between paragraph-level failure-mode detection and the `intent` field
- no piece-level voice aggregate (so compound voice drift where every paragraph clusters at TTR 0.44-0.46 is invisible)
- no session-glossary: decisions on paragraph 3 do not constrain the model's attempt on paragraph 15 (fd-literary-translation-pairing-voice-fidelity)
- no residual channel: paragraphs outside the 5-mode basis are silently marked clean, and the architecture has no mechanism to detect that its constituent set is incomplete (fd-tidal-harmonic-constituent-decomposition)

fd-polynesian-etak-moving-island-model states this most sharply: the v1 schema is structurally a log-of-bearings, not a position estimate. A model trained on a schema that cannot represent cross-paragraph relations cannot develop cross-paragraph judgment from that training data, regardless of training volume.

fd-copy-editing-practice confirms from editorial craft: a real copy editor models (1) what the piece IS, (2) what the piece DOES, (3) what the piece SOUNDS LIKE, then (4) diagnoses passages against the whole-piece model. The current schema flattens 2-4 into 4, which is editorially the same as the detectors-for-rewriting architecture the pivot claims to be leaving behind — just with a richer detector taxonomy.

**Fix:** Before committing Decision 2, add four schema fields:
- `structuralRelations: [{paragraphs: [int], type: enum, reason: string, attempt: string|null}]` — cross-paragraph judgments (merge/split/reorder/cut)
- `voiceProfile: {ttrMean, ttrStdDev, burstinessMean, burstinessStdDev, ...}` — piece-level aggregates (compression of stdDev = flattened voice)
- `modelUncertain: bool, uncertainReason: string` — residual channel for paragraphs that deviate from genre baseline but match no named failure mode
- `sessionGlossary: Map<constructType, "endorsed"|"corrected">` — accumulated per-session constraint context, passed to subsequent paragraphs' LLM calls

Couple `intent` to `diagnostic-context.ts` as a mandatory (not optional) conditioning input for all failure-mode detectors. If `intent` is null, detectors default to flagging ambiguities rather than making false-confident failure-mode calls.

### 4. Non-expert signal contamination has no architectural firewall [3/4 tracks]

**Surfaced by:** fd-hci-assisted-writing (A), fd-quipu-knotkeeper-training-density (C), fd-tidal-estuary-sediment-equilibrium (C), fd-murano-prova-master-apprentice-correction (C), fd-navajo-weaving-apprentice-taste-transmission (D)

**Problem:** T4 acknowledges non-experts may accept uncritically, but Decision 3 commits to a single interaction shape. fd-quipu-knotkeeper-training-density identifies the structural cause: the architecture has no provenance mechanism to maintain the distinction between expert signal (Cuzco-trained keeper) and population noise (village keeper) as volume scales. By volume, non-expert signals will dominate; as the user base grows, Ryan's signal fraction approaches zero, and the classifier converges toward "whatever non-experts silently accept" — which is whatever the classifier's current output is. This is a self-reinforcing feedback loop invisible to per-session metrics.

fd-tidal-estuary-sediment-equilibrium frames it as the depositional-baseline problem: MCP (tidal scouring) continues producing high-quality signal, but the baseline it is calibrating against drifts as Scout (riverine deposition) accumulates. The MCP signal does not degrade; the classifier's prior — what it attempts in the first place — shifts toward non-expert acceptance patterns.

**Fix:** Every training row carries `user_tier: "expert-calibrator" | "expert" | "standard" | "unknown"` and `surface_origin: "mcp-chunk" | "scout-web"`. Minimum defensible policy: Ryan's signal weight 1.0; non-expert passive-accepts weight 0.0 until explicit calibration mechanism is built. Run separate training passes (or explicitly separate training weights) for each surface's data. Periodic held-out evaluation on Ryan's calibration corpus scores MCP-only classifier vs. MCP+Scout-pooled classifier — divergence on the held-out set is the contamination signal. Without this, no post-hoc detection path exists.

### 5. No sample-efficiency target means "improvement" is unfalsifiable [2/4 tracks]

**Surfaced by:** fd-active-learning-signal-design (A), fd-quipu-knotkeeper-training-density (C)

**Problem:** T6 asks "Is 75-150 rows/session enough density?" and "What's the sample-efficiency profile of the planned training method?" The brainstorm names the question but proposes no answer. fd-quipu-knotkeeper-training-density makes this sequentially blocking: training methodology determines sample-efficiency profile, which determines whether 75-150 rows/session is sufficient, and the brainstorm commits to the architecture before answering any of the three. Without per-channel per-failure-mode sample targets, the team cannot distinguish "model is improving" from "model looks different between checkpoints." If the architecture needs 10,000 pairs per mode, that is 65+ Ryan-sessions (~13 weeks) per mode.

**Fix:** Before Phase 3 capture, produce a one-page training-loop specification: (a) training methodology; (b) documented sample-efficiency profile for classifiers of this complexity; (c) minimum sessions-to-first-measurable-improvement at 75-150 rows/session; (d) hold-out 20% of Ryan's historical edits as calibration set with per-failure-mode accuracy targets. If estimated sessions-to-improvement exceeds 20 Ryan-sessions, the architecture must add data augmentation (teacher-model preference pairs) or the build must be scoped down. If this spec cannot be written, the pivot is premature.

### 6. Independent per-paragraph attempts cascade into locally-correct/globally-incoherent outputs [2/4 tracks]

**Surfaced by:** fd-copy-editing-practice (A), fd-polynesian-etak-moving-island-model (C)

**Problem:** Decision 3 implies parallel generation of attempts against the original text. If Ryan accepts paragraph 2's attempt (which restructures a sentence), then accepts paragraph 3's attempt (generated in context of the ORIGINAL paragraph 2), the document now contains a structural discontinuity that was in neither the original nor either individual attempt. Track-changes UI shows this as accept-all success. Ryan does not see incoherence until re-reading.

**Fix:** Attempts must be generated sequentially with rolling context — each attempt conditioned on previously-accepted edits. For Ryan's MCP flow, regenerate directly-affected downstream attempts after each accept event. For Scout batch, either accept this regression explicitly or serialize attempt presentation. Decide in Phase 3 capture.

### 7. No RII-equivalent list — high-consequence edits auto-apply as mechanical [1/4 tracks, P0]

**Surfaced by:** fd-aircraft-maintenance-trust-hierarchy (B)

**Problem:** T8's mechanical/judgment boundary is drawn by difficulty (Vale `autoFix: true`), not by consequence. fd-aircraft-maintenance-trust-hierarchy shows from FAA Part 145 regulations that this exact category error — defining autonomy by action-type instead of by failure consequence — produces the Aloha Airlines pattern: skilled mechanics, simple tasks, catastrophic consequences of error. An `autoFix: true` rule that abbreviates named sources ("Dr. Sarah Chen, former CEO of Meridian Health" → "Dr. Chen") passes the difficulty test but fails the consequence test: attribution change, potential defamation risk, source credibility error, reportorial inaccuracy. The failure is non-recoverable by Ryan after submission.

**Fix:** Define an RII (Required Inspection Item) list as regex patterns that force the judgment tier regardless of model failure-mode classification:
- Any change touching a number (`\b\d[\d,.]*\b`)
- Any change touching a proper noun in a noun phrase
- Any deletion of a title or credential (`\b(Dr|Prof|CEO|CFO|Director|Former|Ex)\b`)
- Any change to a date or year reference

These run as deterministic post-model-output diff checks before tier assignment is finalized. Audit every `autoFix: true` rule in `rule-layers.ts` against consequence matrix — any rule touching proper nouns, numbers, dates, titles goes to judgment tier. This is an afternoon of work, not an architecture change.

### 8. Revision-gate is architecturally invalid for the new output format [1/4 tracks, P0]

**Surfaced by:** fd-nlp-evaluation (A)

**Problem:** `packages/pipeline-worker/src/revision-gate.ts` implements 3-judge pillar scoring calibrated on fully-rewritten documents. The model-then-decide pivot produces a hybrid track-changes artifact (original passages + accepted attempts + user-picked alternatives + user free-text). Applying existing revision-gate to this will produce false negatives: excellent editorial sessions will fail the gate because the scoring rubric expects a particular output shape. The production quality gate silently stops working. The learning loop cannot use revision-gate scores to filter training signal.

**Fix:** Before pivot ships, choose: (a) retire `revision-gate.ts` entirely and replace with per-attempt-quality scoring aggregated to session-level; or (b) keep `revision-gate.ts` for the "all attempts accepted" output only (measurable baseline floor), design separate rubric for hybrid artifact that factors user-decision distribution. Commit in Phase 3 capture.

### 9. Failure-mode detection runs on pre-triaged text — model cannot develop independent detection capability [1/4 tracks]

**Surfaced by:** fd-medical-residency-apprentice-correction (B)

**Problem:** `packages/pipeline-worker/src/prelude.ts` runs Vale and CIPHER before the LLM sees the text. If the model builds its `failureModes` after seeing CIPHER's classification and Vale's flagged issues, it is not detecting `cutSpecifics` independently — it is confirming CIPHER's classification. This is the residency pre-triage deficit: residents from programs with heavy triage support underperform on the "order your own workup" board exam component. The model trains to be a CIPHER-confirmer, never develops the capability to detect `cutSpecifics` on raw input.

**Fix:** For training signal, record both predictions separately: `{rawPrediction: FailureModeModel, preludedPrediction: FailureModeModel}`. The delta is training data for developing prelude-independent detection. Production path can still use prelude-informed predictions.

## Cross-Track Convergence

### Convergence 1 [4/4]: Default-accept destroys training signal

**Already detailed as Critical Finding #1.** Different vocabularies:
- Track A (fd-hci-assisted-writing): "calibration gap" — measured override rate drops to 15-30% of stated-intent
- Track A (fd-active-learning-signal-design): "Goodhart loop on plausibility not correctness"
- Track B (fd-medical-residency-apprentice-correction): "silent agreement problem" — ACGME requires documented concurrence because accept-without-correction is the strongest positive signal the system has; must not be conflated with "never reviewed"
- Track B (fd-radiology-cad-disagreement-loop): "first-reader CAD anchoring bias" — the CAD mark shapes search patterns, not laziness
- Track C (fd-murano-prova-master-apprentice-correction): "silent set-aside" — the Muranese failure mode where the maestro defers diagnosis, producing surface mimicry
- Track C (fd-quipu-knotkeeper-training-density): "no within-expert variance measure" — Ryan's self-agreement rate on re-presented passages bounds the minimum detectable improvement
- Track C (fd-tidal-estuary-sediment-equilibrium): "compound walk-away + non-expert failure mode" — maximum row count, minimum information content
- Track D (fd-navajo-weaving-apprentice-taste-transmission): "no withhold channel" — training signal is always conditioned on an attempt having been made; the model never gets a signal for "considered attempting and chose not to"

**Unified finding:** The architecture treats "signal generated" as equivalent to "signal informative." Every discipline that has operated a similar loop has learned that the two diverge under realistic conditions (fatigue, non-expert users, async review, inertia). Without provenance, dwell-time, engagement-type instrumentation, the three-channel harvest's "3x density" claim is 3x-noise claim.

**Unified fix:** Every training row carries `{user_id, session_id, piece_id, paragraph_idx, attempt_id, action, alternatives_shown, alternative_picked, dwell_ms, queue_position, user_tier, surface_origin, attempt_model_confidence, rules_shown, signal_type: "agreement"|"correction"|"rejection", training_weight, engagement_type}`. This is the minimum schema to make any channel usable for training. Record at decision time; retrofitting is a schema migration that loses all previously-written rows.

### Convergence 2 [4/4]: The v1 model is a log, not a model

**Already detailed as Critical Finding #3.** Different vocabularies:
- Track A (fd-copy-editing-practice): "90% passage-diagnostics, 10% piece gesture — professionally misrepresented as 'model of the piece'"
- Track A (fd-llm-agent-architecture): "implementation will require at least 4 distinct LLM calls with different context requirements; conflating them into 'one big call' guarantees diagnostic-anchored attempts"
- Track B (fd-literary-translation-pairing-voice-fidelity): "missing session glossary — chapter 1 decisions do not constrain chapter 15"
- Track C (fd-polynesian-etak-moving-island-model): "log-of-bearings, not a position estimate — cannot detect internal inconsistency"
- Track D (fd-tidal-harmonic-constituent-decomposition): "no residual channel — architecture commits to never looking at what the 5 constituents don't explain"
- Track D (fd-navajo-weaving-apprentice-taste-transmission): "wrong unit of compositional judgment — paragraph is the typographic unit, not the compositional unit"

**Unified finding:** The architecture optimizes for what is measurable at the paragraph level and structurally forecloses what requires piece-level or cross-paragraph representation. This is the same class of error as pre-Doodson tidal analysts using only M2 and S2. The scope-reduction to paragraph-level is not a neutral v1 simplification — it is a commitment to a training signal that cannot later develop structural or piece-level judgment from its own data.

**Unified fix:** Add the four schema fields (`structuralRelations`, `voiceProfile`, `modelUncertain`, `sessionGlossary`). Couple `intent` to detection as mandatory conditioning. Do not ship v1 claiming "model of the piece" if the schema is 90% passage-diagnostics — either rewrite Decision 2 to include piece-level representation, or explicitly scope v1 as "passage-level only" with documented acknowledgment that structural/register/cross-paragraph pathologies will be systematically missed.

### Convergence 3 [3/4]: The 5 failure modes are not operationally orthogonal

**Surfaced by:** fd-copy-editing-practice (A), fd-nlp-evaluation (A), fd-tidal-harmonic-constituent-decomposition (D)

**Different vocabularies:**
- Track A (fd-nlp-evaluation): "multi-class LLM classifiers on overlapping categories typically show 15-20% within-category confusion"
- Track A (fd-copy-editing-practice): "5 modes omit register failure and structural failure — two of the most common AI-output pathologies in long-form journalism"
- Track D (fd-tidal-harmonic-constituent-decomposition): "flattenedVoice and clicheSwap are near-degenerate tidal constituents — both fire when AI-generated text replaces vivid specific language with smooth generic language; the list was derived from editorial experience not from variance-decomposition of the actual corpus"

**Unified finding:** Ryan's 5 failure modes are plausible starting categories but were never orthogonality-checked. They miss register failure and structural failure (the two most common long-form AI pathologies), they overlap pairwise (flattenedVoice × clicheSwap, cutSpecifics × tooConservative), and every mode can be scored "not detected" by under-editing — so the v1 model actively rewards the `tooConservative` failure mode it claims to detect.

**Unified fix:** Before freezing Decision 2:
1. Run co-occurrence analysis on existing failure-mode labels in the corpus — if flattenedVoice × clicheSwap correlation >0.3, collapse them into compound `voiceErosion` with severity scalar
2. Make failure-mode fields severity scalars (float in [0,1] with null = not detected) instead of booleans — this gives the attempt-engine triage ordering and the training loop regression targets
3. Add a per-piece "editing aggressiveness" counter alongside failure-mode counts — a session with 0 detected failures AND 0 attempts is flagged as suspicious rather than clean
4. Either add register + structural failure modes (making it 7) or explicitly scope v1 as passage-level only

### Convergence 4 [3/4]: The attempt schema lacks the model's reasoning

**Surfaced by:** fd-murano-prova-master-apprentice-correction (C), fd-mars-rover-tactical-sol-planning (D), fd-radiology-cad-disagreement-loop (B)

**Different vocabularies:**
- Track B (fd-radiology-cad-disagreement-loop): "spatial-level disagreement signal — relocation distance quantifies localization error"
- Track C (fd-murano-prova-master-apprentice-correction): "pick-up-the-punty correction — the pedagogical content was the demonstration of the causal chain, not just the result"
- Track D (fd-mars-rover-tactical-sol-planning): "no contingency pre-plan — rejection triggers a restart, not a cascade; attempts carry no `rejectionReasoning` or `contingencyAttempt` fields"

**Unified finding:** The architecture optimizes the happy path (acceptance) and underspecifies the rejection path. When Ryan rejects an attempt, he faces A/B/C alternatives without the model's reasoning about why the attempt was wrong, forcing him to do more cognitive work post-rejection than on acceptance. The training loop inherits this asymmetry: a pick or correction records "model's attempt was wrong" without recording what failure mode triggered the disagreement.

**Unified fix:**
- Attempt schema includes `attemptReasoning: string` (1-2 sentences: what problem this attempt is solving) and `contingencyHint: string` (if this seems wrong, likely X), displayed on rejection only
- After a pick or free-text, one-click "reason for disagreement" selector from the failure-mode set plus "other" — converts pick-delta rows from "B was preferred" into "B was preferred, correcting flattenedVoice"
- Record span-level diff between model attempt and Ryan's correction (computable client-side, no additional Ryan interaction) — CAD's localization signal

### Convergence 5 [3/4]: Cross-session drift has no detection mechanism

**Surfaced by:** fd-medical-residency-apprentice-correction (B), fd-aircraft-maintenance-trust-hierarchy (B), fd-tidal-estuary-sediment-equilibrium (C)

**Different vocabularies:**
- Track B (fd-medical-residency-apprentice-correction): "no M&M conference equivalent — correction signals never aggregate to surface systematic gaps"
- Track B (fd-aircraft-maintenance-trust-hierarchy): "normalization of deviance — per-decision accepts accumulate into document-level drift without a cross-session audit; need C-check/D-check equivalent"
- Track C (fd-tidal-estuary-sediment-equilibrium): "composed-baseline quality has no periodic re-evaluation mechanism — degradation accumulates below per-interaction detection threshold"

**Unified finding:** The learning loop monitors quality per-interaction but has no multi-session quality instrument. A 5% classifier degradation across 20 sessions is invisible in per-session reject rates (which fluctuate 15-25% from within-session variance). Without aggregate pattern detection, the training-signal density question is unanswerable because the team doesn't know what the rows are telling them.

**Unified fix:**
1. Weekly `FailureModeReport` aggregation query over training-signal rows: per mode, `{falsePositiveRate, falseNegativeRate, genreBreakdown, lastImprovedAt}`. Runs scheduled on zklw. Output is the M&M conference agenda.
2. Calibration corpus: 20-30 passages with Ryan's canonical edits, collected during highest-attention sessions. Every 2 weeks, score current classifier against it. Drift >5% from baseline triggers re-calibration session.
3. Monthly normalization-of-deviance audit: take last 30 accepted pieces, compute aggregate `StatisticalProfile`, compare to baseline from first 30. Drift >1.5 SD flags potential accumulated-acceptance drift.

### Convergence 6 [2/4]: No withhold / demonstration training channel

**Surfaced by:** fd-navajo-weaving-apprentice-taste-transmission (D), fd-active-learning-signal-design (A)

**Different vocabularies:**
- Track A (fd-active-learning-signal-design): "detection-gated sampling policy systematically starves signal on false negatives — no detection → no attempt → no training row → no signal to correct the blind spot"
- Track D (fd-navajo-weaving-apprentice-taste-transmission): "no withhold channel — the model never receives a signal for 'considered attempting and chose not to'; grandmother's silence over correct work is load-bearing pedagogy"

**Unified finding:** The three channels are all conditioned on an attempt having been made. The model has no training pressure to leave correct passages alone. Over enough sessions the model learns an asymmetric lesson: surface more attempts (each accepted attempt is positive reward). The piece will be over-marked.

**Unified fix:** Add `withheld_attempts: [{span, failureModeProbs, ryanLeftAlone: bool}]` to session-level training artifact. Also add periodic forced-surfacing: on ~10% of paragraphs (oversampling those where model reports "no failures"), generate an attempt + A/B/C even when detector says clean. Ryan's reject-all response is the only available source of false-negative signal.

### Convergence 7 [2/4]: Voice drift detection is miscoupled

**Surfaced by:** fd-llm-agent-architecture (A), fd-copy-editing-practice (A), fd-literary-translation-pairing-voice-fidelity (B)

**Different vocabularies:**
- Track A (fd-llm-agent-architecture): "LLMs systematically misread raw statistical metrics — treat 'burstiness: 0.4' as quality judgment, then 'correct' voice drift by writing more uniformly"
- Track A (fd-copy-editing-practice): "voiceDrift is orphaned — computed but not routed to any attempt"
- Track B (fd-literary-translation-pairing-voice-fidelity): "flattenedVoice is binary — reviser's correction cannot close the loop because the flag does not identify what was flattened"

**Unified fix:** (1) Never expose raw TTR/burstiness floats to LLM — quantize to categorical tags relative to corpus baseline (`above_baseline|at_baseline|below_baseline`). (2) Couple voiceDrift explicitly to flattenedVoice with evidence field: `flattenedVoice: {detected: bool, evidence: StatisticalDeltaEntry[] | null}` where evidence records which metrics crossed threshold by how much. (3) Post-session back-translation test: `computeStatisticalDelta(original, accepted-aggregate)`, alert if any metric moves >2 SD from original baseline.

## Tradeoff Adjudication (T1-T8)

### T1: Attempt-composes-baseline vs. 04-07 invariant

**Adjudicated by:** fd-hci-assisted-writing, fd-copy-editing-practice, fd-nlp-evaluation, fd-murano-prova-master-apprentice-correction, fd-tidal-estuary-sediment-equilibrium, fd-navajo-weaving-apprentice-taste-transmission, fd-medical-residency-apprentice-correction, fd-aircraft-maintenance-trust-hierarchy

**Consensus:** The softened invariant ("passable IF writer does nothing actively, because defaults apply") is professionally unusual and dangerous. fd-copy-editing-practice: a piece auto-edited by accepting every surfaced decision is "not a copy-edited piece — it is a pre-final machine draft with editor's veto unused." fd-hci-assisted-writing: walk-away-and-come-back IS the zero-interaction floor, and if it is not observably better than current classify-then-rewrite output, the pivot has a floor regression the decision-surface value cannot compensate.

**Resolution:** Revise the invariant. All auto-accepted attempts are "auditable as a composed edit, NOT a copy-edited piece." Surface in UI with banner ("AI's best guess applied wholesale — review before publishing"). Pre-build gate: run one Scout-equivalent batch through simulated "model-then-decide auto-apply all" and measure against current classify-then-rewrite output on Ryan's rubric. If not strictly better, the pivot has a floor regression that must be addressed before ship. Also add `documentLevelAccept` event (fd-aircraft-maintenance-trust-hierarchy) — Ryan submitting the piece as complete is a categorically distinct training signal from accumulated per-decision accepts.

### T2: Confidence calibration is still a thing

**Adjudicated by:** fd-active-learning-signal-design, fd-hci-assisted-writing, fd-radiology-cad-disagreement-loop, fd-medical-residency-apprentice-correction, fd-tidal-harmonic-constituent-decomposition

**Consensus:** The brainstorm is correct to reject confidence-gated surfacing, but fails to replace it with any alternative. fd-radiology-cad-disagreement-loop: CAD solved this not with confidence gating but with sensitivity tuning — per-failure-mode thresholds calibrated from training corpus false-positive rates. fd-medical-residency-apprentice-correction: externally-computed entrustment level (1-5 per failure mode per genre) is not self-referential and replaces the need for internal confidence. fd-tidal-harmonic-constituent-decomposition: severity is distinct from confidence; binary flags conflate them.

**Resolution:**
- Replace boolean failure-mode fields with severity scalars in [0,1] (`null` = not detected)
- Implement per-failure-mode sensitivity dial in `diagnostic-context.ts`: `maxSurfacedPerPiece` per mode per genre, set by corpus calibration not by model confidence
- Compute `entrustmentLevel: 1-5` per attempt from the model's correction rate for that failure mode in that genre (external, not self-reported). UI treatment differs by level without exposing confidence scores.
- For high-uncertainty attempts (multi-mode overlap, or entrustment 1-2), force-review UX: not auto-applied — user must explicitly accept/reject/pick

### T3: Scout / web UI path

**Adjudicated by:** fd-hci-assisted-writing, fd-medical-residency-apprentice-correction, fd-quipu-knotkeeper-training-density, fd-tidal-estuary-sediment-equilibrium, fd-mars-rover-tactical-sol-planning

**Consensus:** The web UI is not just a product strategy question — it is an operational necessity question. fd-mars-rover-tactical-sol-planning: Scout's async-review regime cannot function without triage infrastructure; 200 pieces × 15-30 undifferentiated attempts is telemetry without decisional downlink. fd-hci-assisted-writing: acceptance rate climbs to 100% by item 8-12 in batch review; Scout pieces past item 5 are fatigue-degraded signal.

**Resolution:** The web UI requires a decisional-downlink artifact: a `reviewSummary` field in the model-of-the-piece with top 3-5 highest-severity judgment calls ranked by severity, rendered above-the-fold before the full track-changes document. Scout training signal must be architecturally separated from MCP: `surface_origin` field on every row, separate training runs or weights, held-out calibration evaluation detects cross-surface contamination. Also required: either drop Scout from training signal entirely (`training_weight: 0`) or require minimum engagement per judgment call past item 5 (force-slow throughput loss for signal integrity). Decide before build.

### T4: Non-expert writers

**Adjudicated by:** fd-hci-assisted-writing, fd-quipu-knotkeeper-training-density, fd-tidal-estuary-sediment-equilibrium, fd-murano-prova-master-apprentice-correction, fd-navajo-weaving-apprentice-taste-transmission

**Consensus:** Unified interaction model across expert and non-expert is the primary contamination risk for paying-customer era. fd-hci-assisted-writing: calibration gap in non-experts systematically over-trusts AI outputs in professional-seeming UX regardless of labeling. fd-quipu-knotkeeper-training-density: without tier provenance, as user base grows Ryan's signal fraction approaches zero and the classifier converges toward non-expert acceptance patterns.

**Resolution:** Tier users explicitly at registration. Expert (Ryan + invited editors) → full try-every-time UX, training weight 1.0. Non-expert (paying customers) → force-review UX (no auto-apply, explicit accept required per decision), training weight 0.0 until verified-editorial flag is earned. Sessions with accept-rate >95% flagged as low-trust. Write tier into training-row schema. This preserves the non-expert product without letting their signal contaminate the preference model.

### T5: v1 model coverage

**Adjudicated by:** fd-nlp-evaluation, fd-copy-editing-practice, fd-llm-agent-architecture, fd-polynesian-etak-moving-island-model, fd-tidal-harmonic-constituent-decomposition, fd-navajo-weaving-apprentice-taste-transmission

**Consensus:** The failure-mode-aware schema shifts Goodhart rather than solving it. Every mode has a "not detected = under-edited" path. The 5 modes omit register and structural failures. The model is a log of per-paragraph detections, not a piece-level model.

**Resolution:** See Critical Finding #3 + Convergence 3. Concrete:
- Add severity scalars (not booleans) to failure modes
- Add `modelUncertain` residual channel
- Add `structuralRelations` array at top level
- Add `voiceProfile` piece-level aggregate
- Couple `intent` as mandatory conditioning for all detectors
- Either add register + structural modes or scope v1 explicitly as passage-level only
- Add per-piece editing-aggressiveness counter as Goodhart-resistance metric (0 attempts + 0 failures = suspicious flag)

### T6: Learning loop calibration requires Ryan's time

**Adjudicated by:** fd-active-learning-signal-design, fd-quipu-knotkeeper-training-density, fd-medical-residency-apprentice-correction, fd-nlp-evaluation

**Consensus:** The question is not density but (a) what training method, (b) what sample-efficiency profile for that method, (c) what calibration-corpus target. The three are sequentially blocking. Without answers, density is a village-accounting count being applied to an imperial-pattern-detection question.

**Resolution:** See Critical Finding #5. Non-negotiable pre-build: one-page training-loop spec with training method, per-channel objective, per-mode sample target, held-out calibration protocol. If the spec estimates >20 Ryan-sessions to first measurable improvement, either add data augmentation or scope v1 down.

### T7: The attempt might be the wrong unit

**Adjudicated by:** fd-copy-editing-practice, fd-polynesian-etak-moving-island-model, fd-navajo-weaving-apprentice-taste-transmission, fd-literary-translation-pairing-voice-fidelity, fd-tidal-harmonic-constituent-decomposition

**Consensus:** The attempt unit is wrong — and the consequence of shipping v1 at the wrong unit is that training data is locked to passage-level, structurally foreclosing cross-paragraph judgment. fd-navajo-weaving-apprentice-taste-transmission: scope reductions that exclude signal types from the training loop are irreversible in ways UI scope reductions are not.

**Resolution:** Add `structuralRelations: [{paragraphs: [int], type: "merge"|"split"|"reorder"|"cut", reasoning: string, attempt: string | null}]` at top level of model-of-the-piece. For v1, populate only when failure-mode pass detects high voiceDrift variance across consecutive paragraphs OR when loadBearing elements in consecutive paragraphs reference each other. Cheap v1 population, but critically: training rows start accumulating at structural granularity from day one, so the model is not cold-start for structural judgment when v2 expands scope.

### T8: Mechanical vs. judgment fix boundary

**Adjudicated by:** fd-aircraft-maintenance-trust-hierarchy, fd-llm-agent-architecture, fd-copy-editing-practice, fd-tidal-estuary-sediment-equilibrium, fd-mars-rover-tactical-sol-planning

**Consensus:** Strong convergence across 5 agents, three distinct mechanisms. fd-aircraft-maintenance-trust-hierarchy: consequence-based RII list (the most directly actionable finding in the review — afternoon of work). fd-llm-agent-architecture: mechanical is a closed code enumeration, not an LLM prompt. fd-copy-editing-practice: sentence splitting is ALWAYS a judgment call regardless of length; "grammar" in the broad sense is not mechanical. fd-tidal-estuary-sediment-equilibrium: context-dependent borderline cases need surface-as-judgment default. fd-mars-rover-tactical-sol-planning: operational criteria per instance (confidence + reversibility + impact-if-wrong), not category-level auto-apply.

**Resolution:**
1. Mechanical bucket is a closed code enumeration (TypeScript const in `rule-layers.ts`), strictly: spelling (dictionary), typographic standardization (smart quotes, en/em dashes, hyphens), Vale rules already tagged "deterministic substitution". Everything else — including sentence splitting, clause reordering, "grammar" in the broad sense — is a judgment call by construction.
2. Add RII pattern list that forces judgment-tier regardless of mechanical classification: any change touching numbers, proper nouns in noun phrases, titles/credentials, dates/years.
3. Per-edit confidence + reversibility + impact-if-wrong scoring in `rule-layers.ts` — auto-apply gated on `confidence > 0.95 AND impactIfWrong == 'low'`; everything else surfaces as judgment call (pre-selected for accept convenience, but visible and rejectable).

## Track-Specific Insights

### Domain-Expert Insights (Track A)

**Training-objective specificity (fd-active-learning-signal-design):** Most specialists would reflexively say "three channels, train a preference model." This agent's DPO-compatibility analysis is the single most implementation-critical finding non-specialists could not have surfaced — it converts Decision 4 from "done" to "train on free-text as SFT with rejection-sampling filter, NOT as DPO-preferred completion." Three channels, three distinct training objectives, no blending.

**LLM call-graph isolation (fd-llm-agent-architecture):** The brainstorm treats "model of the piece" as a single conceptual artifact. The implementation requires 4 distinct LLM calls with different context requirements. Conflating them guarantees diagnostic-anchored attempts (model anchors to its own diagnostic vocabulary rather than the piece's voice). Isolation boundary between diagnosis-call and generation-call is the most load-bearing architecture decision; one big schema-emitting call is the wrong choice even if it looks cheapest.

**Evaluation infrastructure precedence (fd-nlp-evaluation):** Evaluation redesign must happen BEFORE the pivot, not in parallel. Existing `revision-gate.ts` is architecturally invalid for the hybrid output format. New failure-mode classifiers have no specified inter-rater reliability target — Krippendorff's alpha on 3-run detection of 10 paragraphs per mode (30 LLM calls, an hour of work) must happen before schema is frozen. Modes with alpha <0.7 cannot be used as hard training labels.

**Editorial craft reality (fd-copy-editing-practice):** The junior/senior editor metaphor misrepresents what the system is. A junior has global comprehension. The AI does not. A junior writes queries for ambiguities, not 15-30 decision points per piece — a real senior reviewing that volume of queries would conclude the junior is unqualified. Either change the framing to "deterministic passage-level reviewer" or re-architect to match the metaphor (piece-level comprehension, consolidated queries).

**HCI UX vs. signal-validity (fd-hci-assisted-writing):** Decision 3 is really three different UX decisions (Ryan-MCP, Ryan-Scout, non-expert) compressed into one. Until Decision 3 decomposes by user/surface tier with explicit per-tier UX primitives and per-tier training weights, the "3x signal density" claim overstates available signal.

### Parallel-Discipline Insights (Track B)

**Silent agreement problem (fd-medical-residency-apprentice-correction):** ACGME requires documented concurrence on accept-without-correction because it is the strongest positive evidence the system has — but only when it carries `dwellMs` and the failure modes that were presented. Add `reviewEvidence` field. Add the M&M-conference scheduled aggregation query on zklw.

**CAD second-reader mode (fd-radiology-cad-disagreement-loop):** First-reader CAD anchors the radiologist's search pattern. Second-reader mode (original first, then attempt on demand) produces systematically higher-quality training rows. Opt-in per session for MCP, flagged in training schema as `{displayMode: "second-reader"}`, weighted higher in calibration. Plus differential weighting: disagreement rows get 3x weight vs. agreement rows (established CAD-FDA training protocol).

**Translation glossary persistence (fd-literary-translation-pairing-voice-fidelity):** The most actionable structural missing piece. Chunk-by-chunk without within-session constraint propagation produces incoherent output even when each chunk is locally correct. `sessionGlossary` accumulator in MCP handler — small ephemeral state, passed as JSON context block to each subsequent paragraph's model call. Plus project-level voice document for A/B/C generation constraint (computed from Ryan's approved edits per author).

**FAA Required Inspection Items (fd-aircraft-maintenance-trust-hierarchy):** The review's most directly actionable finding. T8 is framed as philosophical ("who decides?"); the aviation answer is operational — consequence-based RII list pre-decided by expert review. Audit `autoFix: true` rules in `rule-layers.ts` against consequence matrix. Afternoon of work, closes a catastrophic-failure-mode gap.

### Structural Insights (Track C)

**Diagnosis vs. correction capture (fd-murano-prova-master-apprentice-correction):** The architecture has designed a correction-capture system without a diagnosis-capture system. The pedagogical mechanism in prova was the diagnosis, not the correction. One-click "reason for disagreement" from the failure-mode set converts pick-delta from preference signal to diagnostic signal — without which the failure-mode classifier improves Ryan's pick-prediction while remaining structurally unable to improve its failure-mode detection.

**Position estimate vs. log-of-bearings (fd-polynesian-etak-moving-island-model):** Names exactly what "model of the piece" aspires to be vs. what the v1 schema actually is. A log of per-paragraph detections cannot detect cross-paragraph inconsistency; a position estimate can. The distinction is architectural, not a scope question — no training volume on paragraph-level labels produces cross-paragraph judgment in a schema that has no slot for it.

**Calibration hierarchy (fd-quipu-knotkeeper-training-density):** Signal quality is a property of both channel type AND provenance. The brainstorm decomposes by channel but not by provenance. User-tier + surface-origin provenance must be enforced at schema level, or as the user base grows the classifier converges toward "whatever non-experts silently accept" — which is whatever the classifier already produces. Self-reinforcing feedback loop invisible to per-session metrics. Plus: Ryan's within-expert variance is unmeasured; without a self-agreement test on re-presented passages, the noise floor of the accept/reject channel is unknown and minimum-detectable-improvement is undefined.

**Cross-surface substrate sharing (fd-tidal-estuary-sediment-equilibrium):** Two processes (MCP + Scout) sharing a substrate (training pipeline) are defined by their interaction dynamics, not by the properties of either process alone. Per-surface metrics on MCP cannot detect the drift because Ryan's behavior is unchanged — what changes is the classifier's prior, shaped by the pooled training signal. Held-out evaluation comparing MCP-only vs. MCP+Scout-pooled classifiers on Ryan's calibration corpus is the only contamination detection instrument.

### Frontier Patterns (Track D)

**Residual channel as load-bearing (fd-tidal-harmonic-constituent-decomposition):** The deepest finding in the review. Coastal flood-warning systems monitor residuals, not fitted tides — structured residual reveals incomplete constituent sets, and discarding it kills people. The brainstorm's architecture commits to never looking at what the 5 failure modes don't explain. `modelUncertain: bool` field is a one-line schema change that converts the residual from discarded noise to a prioritized signal channel. Plus: the 5 modes were derived from editorial experience, not from variance-decomposition of the corpus — pairwise correlation analysis is a test that has never been run.

**Spirit-line discipline as prescribed anti-optimization (fd-navajo-weaving-apprentice-taste-transmission):** Diné weaving encodes mandatory imperfection as a rule of the tradition — optimization toward a completeness metric is prescribed to have an escape hatch. Second-order Goodhart: the anti-Goodhart solution (voice metrics as diagnostic) is itself Goodhartable (model learns to preserve metrics rather than voice). Designate TTR and burstiness as `diagnosticOnly: true` — flag passages for Ryan's attention, never score attempts. Plus the withhold channel — demonstration rows ("Ryan left this alone") are as important as correction rows, and the architecture has no slot for them. Without them, the model develops a negative-space definition of quality (everything not rejected) rather than a positive definition; the ceiling is Ryan's rejection threshold, not Ryan's taste.

**Rover decisional downlink (fd-mars-rover-tactical-sol-planning):** The most operationally specific finding from the outer tracks. Systems with latency between actor and decision-maker eventually invent: (a) contingency pre-planning (`attemptReasoning` + `contingencyHint` in the attempt schema, displayed only on rejection); (b) regime-specific interfaces (web UI needs `reviewSummary` triage artifact, not raw track-changes); (c) operational-criteria-based autonomy boundaries (per-instance confidence + reversibility + impact, not action-type categorization). Mars rovers had to invent all three on day one because of the 20-minute comm delay; Scout's async-review regime faces the same constraint. Plus: keep-out zones for `loadBearing` spans — hard exclusions registered in the attempt-engine before any attempt generation, not relying on `cutSpecifics` detector post-hoc.

## Architectural Recommendations (Ordered)

Lead with changes that, if not made, will cause the architecture to collapse.

### P0 — Collapse Risks (must resolve before Phase 3 capture)

1. **Full training-row schema with provenance, engagement, and signal-type columns — recorded from day one.** Convergence 1 + 4. Fields: `{user_id, session_id, piece_id, paragraph_idx, attempt_id, action, alternatives_shown, alternative_picked, dwell_ms, queue_position, user_tier, surface_origin, signal_type: "agreement"|"correction"|"rejection", training_weight, engagement_type, attempt_model_confidence, rules_shown, display_mode}`. Effort: 1 day schema + instrumentation. Depends-on: nothing. Retrofit is lossy — every row written before this fix is in is permanently unusable for quality-weighted training.

2. **One-page training-loop specification.** Critical Finding #5. Commit to training methodology per channel, per-mode sample-efficiency target, held-out calibration protocol, 20% held-out from Ryan's historical edits. Effort: 2-3 days research + writing. Depends-on: Recommendation #1 schema. If spec cannot be written, pivot is premature.

3. **Separate MCP and Scout training at the data-pool level.** Critical Finding #4 + Convergence T3/T4. Separate training runs OR explicit separate weights. Held-out calibration evaluation on Ryan's corpus compares MCP-only vs. MCP+Scout-pooled classifiers. Effort: 1 day data-plumbing + 1 day evaluation-script scaffold. Depends-on: Recommendation #1 (surface_origin field exists).

4. **Decision 2 schema additions — before build.** Convergence 2 + T5. Four fields: `structuralRelations[]`, `voiceProfile` piece-level aggregate, `modelUncertain: bool`, `sessionGlossary` accumulator. Make `intent` mandatory conditioning for all detectors in `diagnostic-context.ts`. Replace boolean failure modes with severity scalars `[0,1]` (null = not detected). Effort: 2 days schema + prompt updates. Depends-on: nothing.

5. **RII list audit of `rule-layers.ts`.** Critical Finding #7 + T8. Deterministic post-model-output diff check for numbers, proper nouns, titles, dates. Any `autoFix: true` rule touching these moves to judgment tier. Mechanical bucket becomes closed code enumeration (TypeScript const), not LLM judgment. Effort: afternoon. Depends-on: nothing.

6. **Revision-gate redesign decision.** Critical Finding #8 + T1. Choose: retire entirely with per-attempt scoring OR keep for "all attempts accepted" output only with separate hybrid-artifact rubric. Before ship. Effort: 1-2 days to decide + scope; full redesign is separate work. Depends-on: Decision 2 schema finalized.

### P1 — Architectural Improvements (needed but not collapse-inducing)

7. **Isolated-context attempt generation.** fd-llm-agent-architecture. Attempt call receives only `{paragraph_text, failure_mode_tag, piece_intent}` — NOT the full model-of-the-piece JSON. Run contamination test (10 pieces, blind-rated shared-vs-isolated conditions) before locking decision. Effort: 1 day test + prompt refactor. Depends-on: #4.

8. **Sequential attempt generation with rolling context.** Critical Finding #6. Each attempt conditioned on previously-accepted edits. For MCP, regenerate directly-affected downstream attempts after each accept event. Effort: 2-3 days (session state + regeneration logic). Depends-on: #1, #4.

9. **Per-failure-mode sensitivity dial in `diagnostic-context.ts`.** fd-radiology-cad-disagreement-loop + T2. `maxSurfacedPerPiece` per mode per genre, calibrated from corpus false-positive rates. Requires one-time calibration pass (20 pieces from Ryan's edited corpus). Effort: 1 day calibration + 1 day implementation. Depends-on: #4 (severity scalars).

10. **Withhold-attempt channel + forced-surfacing.** Convergence 6. Log `withheld_attempts: [{span, failureModeProbs, ryanLeftAlone}]` to session artifact. Force-surface attempt + A/B/C on 10% of "clean" paragraphs (budget against Ryan's throughput). Effort: 1 day logging + 1 day UX. Depends-on: #1.

11. **Attempt schema reasoning + contingency + reason-for-disagreement.** Convergence 4. `attemptReasoning`, `contingencyHint` generated per attempt (collapsed UI, expanded on rejection). Post-pick/free-text one-click reason selector. Span-level diff between attempt and correction logged client-side. Effort: 2 days prompts + UI + schema. Depends-on: #1.

12. **Non-expert tier UX separation.** T4 + Convergence 1. Expert tier: try-every-time auto-apply, weight 1.0. Non-expert tier: force-review (no auto-apply, explicit accept per decision), weight 0.0 until verified-editorial flag. Sessions with accept-rate >95% flagged low-trust. Effort: 1-2 days UX + tier logic. Depends-on: #1 (user_tier field).

13. **Decisional-downlink `reviewSummary` for web UI.** Convergence T3 + fd-mars-rover-tactical-sol-planning. Top 3-5 highest-severity judgment calls ranked by severity scalar, above-the-fold card in web UI. Effort: 1-2 days. Depends-on: #4 (severity scalars), #9.

14. **Second-reader display mode for MCP calibration sessions.** fd-radiology-cad-disagreement-loop. Opt-in per session. Training rows flagged `{displayMode: "second-reader"}`, weighted higher in calibration. Effort: 1 day MCP tool change. Depends-on: #1.

### P2 — Observability + Ongoing Quality

15. **Weekly FailureModeReport aggregation on zklw.** Convergence 5 + fd-medical-residency-apprentice-correction. Per-mode `{falsePositiveRate, falseNegativeRate, genreBreakdown, lastImprovedAt}`. Scheduled job. Effort: 1 day query + cron. Depends-on: #1.

16. **Monthly normalization-of-deviance audit.** fd-aircraft-maintenance-trust-hierarchy. Last 30 accepted pieces vs. baseline-first 30 via `StatisticalProfile`. Drift >1.5 SD flag. Effort: 1 day. Depends-on: nothing new.

17. **Calibration corpus with 2-week held-out evaluation.** fd-tidal-estuary-sediment-equilibrium. 20-30 passages with Ryan's canonical edits. Drift >5% from baseline triggers re-calibration session. Effort: half day curation + 1 day eval script. Depends-on: #2.

18. **Ryan self-agreement test.** fd-quipu-knotkeeper-training-density. Periodic re-presentation of 5-10 previously-rated passages without labels. Measures noise floor. Effort: half day UX + half day analysis. Depends-on: #1.

19. **Inter-run reliability test for failure-mode detection.** fd-nlp-evaluation. 3 runs per paragraph on 10 paragraphs per mode, Krippendorff's alpha. Modes <0.7 cannot be hard labels. One hour of compute. Depends-on: #4 (schema).

20. **5-mode co-occurrence analysis.** Convergence 3 + fd-tidal-harmonic-constituent-decomposition. Pairwise correlation on labels in corpus. Collapse modes with >0.3 correlation into compound constituents. Effort: half day script. Depends-on: nothing new.

## What Holds Up

The following pass adversarial review and can proceed:

- **Decision 1 (Scope — Option C "Invert"):** Concurred by fd-copy-editing-practice, fd-llm-agent-architecture, fd-medical-residency-apprentice-correction, fd-mars-rover-tactical-sol-planning. Multiple agents explicitly endorsed abandoning silent rewrite as the correct scope choice. The explicit rejection of options A and B holds up — middle-path approaches would contaminate the learning signal and not address the pipeline-is-wrong-shape concern.

- **Hard Constraint: Elicitation is the product.** No agent contested this. fd-mars-rover-tactical-sol-planning frames the entire rover operations experience as structurally confirming this — for asynchronous-review regimes, the decisional-downlink artifact (not the raw telemetry) is the product.

- **Hard Constraint: No "recommended" label.** Agent findings refine this rather than contest it. fd-hci-assisted-writing notes the label does not prevent anchoring (which the brainstorm does not claim — it claims trust hasn't been earned yet, which is correct). The fix is display-order randomization and position-bias correction, not reverting to "recommended."

- **Hard Constraint: No Opus in bulk runs.** Irrelevant to most agents; those that addressed compute cost (fd-llm-agent-architecture) endorsed prompt-caching at model-of-the-piece level and cheap short attempt calls. Cost envelope appears tractable.

- **Ryan's 04-07 "internal prediction" invariant.** fd-active-learning-signal-design flags that internal-prediction-vs-user-pick delta is a distinct fourth training signal that the brainstorm conflates with the three channels. But the invariant itself — that prediction must be recorded — is validated as structurally necessary, not questioned.

- **Decision 4's three-channel signal topology.** The channels themselves are the right channels. What's broken is (a) missing provenance + weight columns, (b) DPO-incompatibility of free-text, (c) absence of withhold channel. None of these require rethinking the three channels — they require extending the schema around them.

- **Commitment to junior-editor framing for internal model self-conception.** fd-medical-residency-apprentice-correction confirms the pedagogical metaphor is correct. The refinements (silent agreement documentation, M&M aggregation, entrustment-level tracking, write-ups on rejection) extend the metaphor rather than reject it. The framing mismatch identified by fd-copy-editing-practice and fd-murano-prova-master-apprentice-correction applies to the USER-facing UX framing (which needs correction), not to the internal model's self-conception.

## Synthesis Assessment

**Overall quality of the brainstorm:** High on UX thesis clarity; medium on architectural specificity; low on training-loop substrate. The committed decisions (1-4) are well-framed and Decision 1's scope is correct. The v1 schema (Decision 2) is under-specified; the interaction shape (Decision 3) compresses three distinct user regimes into one; the training harvest (Decision 4) lists channels without any training-objective or weighting specification. The brainstorm is self-aware enough to list 8 open tradeoffs, but 6 of the 8 have resolutions already suggested by one or more agents — the tradeoffs are more tractable than their unresolved framing suggests.

**Highest-leverage single improvement:** Full training-row schema with provenance, engagement, dwell-time, and signal-type columns recorded from day one. This is the single change that, if not made before any training rows are written, permanently degrades all subsequent data. Every other architectural improvement can be made later with higher cost but without data loss; this one cannot. It is simultaneously the smallest effort (1 day) and the highest leverage. It is the gating recommendation for Phase 3 capture.

**Most surprising finding:** The residual channel from fd-tidal-harmonic-constituent-decomposition. The insight that storm-surge monitoring systems watch residuals not fitted signals, because residuals reveal incomplete constituent sets, is the one reframe no single adjacent-domain agent would produce. The brainstorm worries about Goodhart at the failure-mode detection level (T5) but does not realize that the architecture commits to never looking at what the 5 failure modes don't explain. `modelUncertain: bool` is a one-field schema change that converts discarded noise into the signal that reveals when the mode set itself is wrong. Equally surprising but second-place: the aircraft-maintenance RII list from fd-aircraft-maintenance-trust-hierarchy, which converts T8 from unresolved philosophy to an afternoon of deterministic regex work.

**Semantic distance value:** The outer tracks earned their cost. Track C's quipu-keeper (provenance hierarchy) and etak (position vs. log) findings are not available from Tracks A or B — they require the structural isomorphism perspective of non-Western knowledge systems that operated at scale without modern instrumentation. Track D's tidal-harmonic residual analysis and Mars rover contingency pre-planning are genuinely novel frames that reframe Decision 2 and T3 respectively in ways no specialist would produce. Conversely, fd-navajo-weaving's ch'ihónít'i spirit-line is more restatement than new insight (the Goodhart-on-metrics concern appears in Tracks A and D), and fd-murano-prova's diagnosis-capture finding converges substantially with fd-medical-residency's M&M aggregation — the tradition-specific framings overlap more than they reveal new mechanisms. Overall: 4/7 outer-track agents produced findings that alter the architectural recommendations; 3/7 restated inner-track findings in novel vocabulary. That ratio justifies the outer-track investment — the "never would have thought of" category has three entries (residual channel, position-vs-log, RII list), each in a different track, each changing the recommendation list.

7	---
8	
9	# Texturaize — Model-Then-Decide Architectural Pivot
10	
11	## What We're Building
12	
13	A pivot from **classify-then-rewrite** (Vale detects AI-isms → LLM rewrites → regex post-processes) to **model-then-decide** (main pass produces a structured model of the piece → writer makes decisions against that model, not against a rewritten draft).
14	
15	Every judgment call the model surfaces gets both **an attempt** (the model's actual edit, labeled *"model's current take"* — not "recommended") and **alternatives** (A/B/C + free-text, one click away). The writer reviews in an inline track-changes UI with the attempt applied as default; engagement happens where the writer disagrees.
16	
17	The AI stops being an author producing text and becomes a junior editor submitting work to a senior editor (Ryan). The junior learns by being corrected.
18	
19	## Why This Approach
20	
21	The shipped classify-then-rewrite pipeline plateaus at "bland without the banned words" — Goodhart's Law on AI-ism count. A detector that counts tokens can never signal whether a paragraph has a point only this writer could have made.
22	
23	Three prior explorations converged on this shape without any single one acting on it structurally:
24	
25	- **2026-04-07 Ryan transcript (Notion):** *"I'm imagining being an editor querying an author... did you mean A, B, or C?"* + *"If you don't let the model guess, even privately, even wrongly, there's no mechanism for the model to improve."*
26	- **2026-04-11 pipeline-architecture-alternatives synthesis:** 16 agents independently surfaced model-then-decide primitives (Ifa classify-then-correct, Quipu passage-level diagnostics, Qulliq proportional ladder, Ottoman moral hazard).
27	- **2026-04-16 ship-for-ryan-first-principles brief:** *"Is the pipeline the product, or an implementation detail?"*
28	
29	This brainstorm's distinctive commitment: **the main pass stops producing a rewritten baseline at all.** The AI either attempts + surfaces alternatives, or asks. It does not silently rewrite.
30	
31	The 2026-04-19 multi-track review (16 agents, 4 tracks) confirmed Decision 1's scope as correct, exposed specific architectural gaps in Decisions 2/3/4, and surfaced three day-zero commitments that become permanently lossy if deferred.
32	
33	## Key Decisions
34	
35	### D1: Scope — Option C "Invert"
36	
37	The main pass produces a structured model of the piece, not a rewritten draft. All judgment calls become decisions surfaced via the elicitation UX. "AI rewrites for you" goes away; "AI attempts + shows alternatives" takes its place.
38	
39	**Review concurrence:** 4 agents across 3 tracks explicitly endorsed abandoning silent rewrite as the correct scope. Middle-path approaches (confidence-gated hybrid, baseline + model on top) would contaminate the learning signal and fail to address the pipeline-is-wrong-shape concern.
40	
41	### D2: v1 Model Shape — Failure-Mode Aware (Revised per Review)
42	
43	The model of the piece at v1 contains:
44	
45	```
46	{
47	  intent: string,                            // 1-2 sentence summary — mandatory conditioning input
48	  paragraphs: [
49	    {
50	      idx: int,
51	      failureModes: {                        // severity scalars [0,1], null = not detected
52	        cutSpecifics:     { severity: float | null, specifics: [string] },
53	        flattenedVoice:   { severity: float | null, evidence: StatisticalDelta[] },
54	        clicheSwap:       { severity: float | null, phrase: string },
55	        contextBlindRule: { severity: float | null },
56	        tooConservative:  { severity: float | null }
57	      },
58	      loadBearing:      [string],            // numbers, names, attributions — RII-protected
59	      voiceDrift:       { ttr, burstiness, mattr50, ... } | null,
60	      modelUncertain:   bool,                // residual channel — paragraph deviates but matches no mode
61	      uncertainReason:  string | null
62	    }
63	  ],
64	  structuralRelations: [                     // cross-paragraph judgments
65	    { paragraphs: [int], type: "merge"|"split"|"reorder"|"cut", reasoning: string, attempt: string | null }
66	  ],
67	  voiceProfile: {                            // piece-level aggregate (detects compound voice drift)
68	    ttrMean: float, ttrStdDev: float,
69	    burstinessMean: float, burstinessStdDev: float
70	  },
71	  sessionGlossary: Map<constructType, "endorsed"|"corrected">,  // accumulated MCP session context
72	  ambiguity: [
73	    { span: string, type: enum, alternatives: [A, B, C] }
74	  ]
75	}
76	```
77	
78	Changes from the initial v1 sketch, driven by review:
79	
80	- **Severity scalars** replace boolean failure-mode flags (fd-nlp-evaluation, fd-tidal-harmonic-constituent-decomposition). Gives attempt-engine triage ordering and training-loop regression targets.
81	- **`modelUncertain` residual channel** (fd-tidal-harmonic-constituent-decomposition). Reveals when the 5-mode basis itself is incomplete. One-field schema change converts discarded noise into the signal that falsifies the mode taxonomy.
82	- **`structuralRelations[]`** (fd-polynesian-etak-moving-island-model, fd-copy-editing-practice). v1 populates only when voice-drift variance is high across consecutive paragraphs OR load-bearing elements cross-reference. Cheap v1 population but training rows start accumulating at structural granularity from day one — prevents cold-start for v2 structural judgment.
83	- **`voiceProfile` piece-level aggregate** (fd-navajo-weaving-apprentice-taste-transmission). Detects compound voice drift where every paragraph clusters at the same TTR band — invisible at per-paragraph level.
84	- **`sessionGlossary`** (fd-literary-translation-pairing-voice-fidelity). Chunk-by-chunk editing without within-session constraint propagation produces incoherent output. Accumulator passed to subsequent paragraphs' LLM calls as context.
85	- **`intent` as mandatory conditioning** for all failure-mode detectors in `diagnostic-context.ts`. If `intent` is null, detectors default to flagging ambiguities rather than making false-confident failure-mode calls.
86	
87	### D3: Interaction Shape — Try-Every-Time with Tier-Split UX
88	
89	**Expert tier (Ryan + invited editors):**
90	Try-every-time auto-apply default. Inline track-changes with attempt applied; accept is one-click ignore. Alternatives (A/B/C + free-text) one click away. Training weight 1.0.
91	
92	**Non-expert tier (paying customers):**
93	Force-review UX. No auto-apply. Writer must explicitly accept/reject/pick per decision. Training weight 0.0 until verified-editorial flag is earned (sessions with accept-rate >95% flagged low-trust).
94	
95	**Why tier-split (review finding):** fd-quipu-knotkeeper-training-density + fd-hci-assisted-writing converge on non-expert signal contamination as the primary risk for paying-customer era. Unified interaction produces a training pool where Ryan's fraction approaches zero as user base grows; classifier converges toward "whatever non-experts silently accept." Separation at the data-pool level is non-negotiable.
96	
97	### D4: Training Harvest — Per-Channel Objectives with Full Provenance
98	
99	Three channels, three distinct training objectives, no blending into a single preference-pair pipeline:
100	
101	| Channel | Signal | Training objective |
102	|---|---|---|
103	| Accept/reject on attempt | Binary classification | Bradley-Terry reward model on (attempt, original-passage) pair |
104	| Pick delta on alternatives | Contrastive ranking | DPO pair (both completions in-distribution by construction) |
105	| Free-text correction | Supervised (exact target) | SFT with rejection-of-attempt as negative in separate loss head OR rejection-sampled SFT filtered to corrections a teacher can approximately reproduce |
106	
107	**Why per-channel (review finding):** fd-active-learning-signal-design identified that naive DPO pairing with free-text corrections as "preferred" produces mode collapse — free-text is by construction out-of-distribution for DPO's implicit reward model. This is single-track but load-bearing; without it, the richest signal channel is silently discarded or poisons the others.
108	
109	Add a **withhold-attempt channel** (fd-navajo-weaving-apprentice-taste-transmission + fd-active-learning-signal-design): log `withheld_attempts: [{span, failureModeProbs, ryanLeftAlone: bool}]` per session. Also force-surface attempt + A/B/C on ~10% of paragraphs even when detector reports clean — Ryan's "reject all" response is the only available false-negative signal.
110	
111	### D5 (NEW): Day-Zero Commitments That Are Permanently Lossy If Deferred
112	
113	These must be implemented before any training row is written. Retrofitting loses all previously-recorded data.
114	
115	#### D5a: Full training-row schema with provenance
116	
117	```
118	{
119	  user_id, session_id, piece_id, paragraph_idx, attempt_id,
120	  action: "accept"|"reject"|"pick"|"free_text"|"withhold",
121	  alternatives_shown: [string], alternative_picked: string | null,
122	  dwell_ms: int, queue_position: int,
123	  user_tier: "expert-calibrator"|"expert"|"standard"|"unknown",
124	  surface_origin: "mcp-chunk"|"scout-web",
125	  signal_type: "agreement"|"correction"|"rejection",
126	  training_weight: float,
127	  engagement_type: "engaged"|"fatigue_accept"|"none",
128	  attempt_model_confidence: float,
129	  rules_shown: [string],
130	  display_mode: "first-reader"|"second-reader",
131	  internal_prediction: string      // Ryan's 04-07 internal-prediction invariant
132	}
133	```
134	
135	Fatigue-accept detection: `dwell_ms < 2000 AND failure_mode_severity > 0.3 AND surface_origin == "scout-web" AND queue_position > 7` → demoted to untrusted-accept bucket.
136	
137	#### D5b: RII (Required Inspection Items) regex list
138	
139	Deterministic post-model-output diff check. Any change matching these patterns forces judgment tier regardless of model's failure-mode classification or mechanical-fix designation:
140	
141	- Any change touching a number: `\b\d[\d,.]*\b`
142	- Any change touching a proper noun in a noun phrase
143	- Any deletion/change of a title or credential: `\b(Dr|Prof|CEO|CFO|Director|Former|Ex)\b`
144	- Any change to a date or year reference
145	
146	Audit every `autoFix: true` rule in `packages/pipeline-worker/src/layers/*.ts` against this consequence matrix. Any rule touching RII patterns moves to judgment tier. Afternoon of work.
147	
148	#### D5c: Mechanical bucket as closed code enumeration
149	
150	Mechanical fixes = closed TypeScript const (not LLM judgment): spelling (dictionary), typographic standardization (smart quotes, en/em dashes), Vale rules already tagged `deterministic substitution`. Everything else — including sentence splitting, clause reordering, and "grammar" in the broad sense — is a judgment call by construction.
151	
152	## Hard Constraints (Unchanged)
153	
154	- Elicitation is the product (confirmed by all tracks).
155	- No "recommended" label (confirmed; refined by adding display-order randomization to prevent position-bias).
156	- Chunk-by-chunk paragraph-level editing, primary interface MCP in Claude Code.
157	- No user-configurable de-AI toggles.
158	- Run AI/ML on zklw. No Opus in bulk runs. Corpus server-side only.
159	- BERT edit-vectors invalid — do not revisit vector-space edit categorization.
160	
161	## Open Questions (For Phase 1.3 / Planning)
162	
163	These are unresolved at brainstorm level. Phase 1.3 dialogue or the plan itself will adjudicate them.
164	
165	**Q1: Training-loop specification (pre-build blocker).** Per-channel training method, per-mode sample-efficiency profile, minimum sessions-to-first-measurable-improvement at 75-150 rows/session, held-out calibration protocol with 20% of Ryan's historical edits. If the spec estimates >20 Ryan-sessions to first measurable improvement, either add data augmentation (teacher-model preference pairs) or scope v1 down. **If this spec cannot be written, the pivot is premature.**
166	
167	**Q2: Register + structural failure modes.** The 5 failure modes omit register failure and structural failure — two of the most common AI long-form pathologies (fd-copy-editing-practice). Options: (a) expand to 7 modes (requires orthogonality check), (b) explicitly scope v1 as passage-level only with documented acknowledgment that structural/register pathologies are systematically missed.
168	
169	**Q3: 5-mode orthogonality check.** Pairwise correlation analysis on existing failure-mode labels in corpus. Modes with >0.3 correlation (candidate: flattenedVoice × clicheSwap) collapse into compound constituents. Half-day script.
170	
171	**Q4: Sequential vs. parallel attempt generation.** Independent per-paragraph attempts against original text cascade into locally-correct/globally-incoherent outputs (fd-copy-editing-practice, fd-polynesian-etak-moving-island-model). For MCP: sequential with rolling context (regenerate downstream attempts after accept). For Scout: accept regression explicitly or serialize attempt presentation. Decide in plan.
172	
173	**Q5: Revision-gate redesign.** `packages/pipeline-worker/src/revision-gate.ts` is architecturally invalid for hybrid attempt-composed output. Choose: (a) retire entirely, replace with per-attempt-quality scoring aggregated to session-level; (b) keep for "all attempts accepted" output only, design separate rubric for hybrid artifact.
174	
175	**Q6: LLM call-graph isolation.** The "model of the piece" cannot be one LLM call producing all fields — diagnostic-anchored attempts result from shared context (fd-llm-agent-architecture). Minimum isolation boundary: attempt generation receives only `{paragraph_text, failure_mode_tag, piece_intent}` — NOT the full model-of-the-piece JSON. Plan must specify call-graph with isolation test (10 pieces, blind-rated shared-vs-isolated conditions) before locking.
176	
177	**Q7: Inter-run reliability target for failure-mode detection.** 3 runs per paragraph on 10 paragraphs per mode, Krippendorff's alpha. Modes with alpha <0.7 cannot be used as hard training labels. One hour of compute, blocks D2 freeze.
178	
179	**Q8: Second-reader mode opt-in for MCP calibration sessions.** fd-radiology-cad-disagreement-loop. Original first, attempt on demand. Higher-quality training rows; flagged in schema as `{display_mode: "second-reader"}`, weighted higher in calibration. Decide whether to ship v1 with first-reader-only or include second-reader opt-in.
180	
181	## Review Summary
182	
183	Full adversarial review at: `docs/research/flux-review/model-then-decide-invert/2026-04-19-synthesis.md`
184	
185	**4/4-track convergent findings (strongest signal):**
186	1. Default-accept without instrumentation destroys training signal → D5a resolves.
187	2. v1 schema was a log, not a model → D2 revisions resolve.
188	
189	**3/4-track convergences:**
190	- 5 modes not orthogonality-checked → Q3.
191	- Attempt schema lacks model's reasoning → covered by attempt-reasoning + contingency-hint additions (implementation detail).
192	- Cross-session drift has no detection mechanism → weekly `FailureModeReport` on zklw, monthly normalization-of-deviance audit (implementation detail).
193	
194	**What holds up (no rethink needed):** Decision 1 scope, elicitation-is-product, no-recommendation stance, three-channel topology, junior-editor framing for internal model self-conception, Ryan's 04-07 internal-prediction invariant.
195	
196	**Highest-leverage single change:** D5a (training-row schema). 1 day of work; retrofitting is architecturally impossible to recover from.
197	
198	**Most surprising finding:** D2's `modelUncertain` residual channel — one-line schema change that converts discarded noise into the signal that reveals when the 5-mode taxonomy itself is wrong.
199	
200	**Most directly actionable:** D5b RII list — afternoon of regex work converts T8 from open philosophy to deterministic post-check.
201	
202	## Relevant Code
203	
204	- `packages/pipeline-worker/src/pipeline.ts` — orchestrator; rewire around model-of-piece output
205	- `packages/pipeline-worker/src/prelude.ts` — parallel deterministic tools; add `StatisticalProfile` piece-aggregate + session glossary handling
206	- `packages/pipeline-worker/src/rule-layers.ts` — audit `autoFix: true` against D5b RII matrix
207	- `packages/pipeline-worker/src/elicitation-detect.ts` — current elicitation entry point; extend to typed failure-mode detection + attempt generation
208	- `packages/pipeline-worker/src/statistical-profile.ts` — already computes 13 metrics; extend to piece-level aggregate for `voiceProfile`
209	- `packages/pipeline-worker/src/diagnostic-context.ts` — add `intent` as mandatory conditioning input
210	- `packages/pipeline-worker/src/analyst-verify.ts` — candidate for isolated-context attempt generation per Q6
211	- `packages/pipeline-worker/src/intake-question.ts` — extend to generate A/B/C alternatives, not single question
212	- `packages/pipeline-worker/src/revision-gate.ts` — resolve Q5 before ship
213	
214	## Prior Research
215	
216	- `docs/research/flux-review/ship-for-ryan-first-principles/first-principles-brief.md` (2026-04-16)
217	- `docs/research/flux-review/pipeline-architecture-alternatives/2026-04-11-synthesis.md`
218	- `docs/research/flux-review/agent-editorial-architecture/2026-04-07-synthesis.md`
219	- `docs/research/flux-review/model-then-decide-invert/2026-04-19-synthesis.md` (this brainstorm's review)
220	- Notion: *2026-04-07 — Editorial UX & Training Signal Architecture* (Ryan transcript — canonical UX spec)
221	- Notion: *Editorial Review Manual: How to Improve the Model* (2026-04-13)

---
artifact_type: brainstorm
